Have they not then travelled on land and seen for themselves what end those before them met with? And the abode of the Hereafter is certainly better for those who fear Allah. Do you not understand this?
translations.12:110: The Messengers continued propagating the divine Message to the people until the Messengers despaired and reckoned that the people would not believe them. Then did Our help come to them and every person, whom We pleased, was saved. And Our punishment was not dispensed with for the sinners.
translations.12:111: In their annals there is certainly a lesson for those endowed with insight. This is not a fabricated hadeeth , but a confirmation of what has preceded it and a detailed explanation of all things and a guide and a mercy to people who believe.
translations.13:1: Alif Lam Meem Ra. Those are Verses of the Book . And that which is revealed to you from your Lord is the truth, but most people believe not.
translations.13:2: Allah is the One Who raised the heavens without any visible support. He then established Himself on the Throne . And He made the sun and the moon subservient. Each pursues its course till an appointed time. He is the Commander and the Governor. He explains the Verses/signs in detail so that you may be certain of meeting your Lord.
translations.13:3: And He is the One Who has stretched the earth to make mountains and rivers therein. And of every kind of fruit He made a pair mate therein. He draws the night as a veil over the day. Indeed, there are signs in that for people who reflect.
translations.13:4: And on the earth there are tracts side by side with gardens of grapes and corn and palm trees – having one common root and others having distinct roots – all irrigated with same water. And yet We make some of them excel others in flavour. There are signs indeed in that for people who understand.
translations.13:5: And if you would consider anything as strange, then strange it is that they say, “When we turn to dust, shall we even then be there in a new creation?” These are they, who suppress the Truth regarding their Lord. And they shall have chains round their necks, and they shall be the inmates of the Fire. In it they shall ever be.
translations.13:6: And they ask you to hasten what is not good rather than what is good, and there certainly have been examples of those before them . And indeed, your Lord is full of forgiveness for people, notwithstanding their wrongdoings. And indeed, your Lord is severe in punishment.
translations.13:7: And those who suppress the Truth say, “Why has not a sign been sent down upon him from his Lord?” You are only a warner and, for every people, a guide.
translations.13:8: Allah knows what any female conceives, and what makes the wombs shrink and what makes them swell. And everything with Him is measured.
translations.13:9: Allah has the knowledge of the unseen and the seen. HE is the One Who is Great, the One Who is High/Exalted.
translations.13:10: The one among you who speaks secretively, the one who speaks openly, the one who hides by night and the one who moves about freely by day – they are all the same for Allah; nothing can escape His notice.
translations.13:11: For every human being there are angels who take turns, in front of him/her and behind, to guard him/her by Allah's command. Allah does indeed not change the condition of a people until they themselves change it. And when Allah wills adversity on a people, there is no averting it. And besides Him they have no ruling authority to defend and take care of them.
translations.13:12: He it is Who shows you the lightning that causes both fear and hope. And He it is Who brings up the heavy cloud.
translations.13:13: And the thunder proclaims His glory with His praise, and the angels stand in awe of Him. And He sends the thunderbolts and hits with them whom He wills. And they quarrel among themselves about Allah, whereas He is extremely powerful.
translations.13:14: To Him is the true prayer! And those whom they pray to besides Allah give them no response on anything. It is just like one stretching his two hands out for water to reach his mouth, but it reaches not. And the prayer of those who suppress the Truth is nothing but an illusion.
translations.13:15: And to Allah does prostrate everything in the heavens and the earth, willingly and unwillingly, as their shadows do morning and evening.
translations.13:16: Ask, “Who is the Lord of the heavens and the earth?” Say, “Allah.” Ask, “Do you take then, besides Him, awliya who have no control over any profit and loss to themselves?” Ask, “Are the blind and those able to see alike? Or can the darknesses and the light be equal? Or have they set up gods besides Allah, who have made creation like He has, and, as a result, the creation creates confusion for them?” Say, “Allah is the Creator of all things. And He is the One and Only, the Almighty!”
translations.13:17: He sends down water from the sky. And valleys flow with water as per their given measure. And the stream bears the swelling froth. And from what they melt in the fire for making ornaments or apparatus arises similar froth. Thus, does Allah compare truth with untruth. Then, as for the froth, it just passes away as a worthless thing. And as for that which profits the people, it remains on the earth. Thus, does Allah explain things giving examples.
translations.13:18: The good is for those who respond to their Lord. And as for those who do not respond to Him, had they all that is in the earth and the like thereof with it, they would certainly offer it for a ransom. An evil reckoning shall await them, and their abode shall be Hell – the worst place to rest in.
translations.13:19: Is he who knows that what has been revealed to you from your Lord is the truth, then, like him who is blind? Only those endowed with insight will ponder on this,
translations.13:20: Those that fulfill the promise to Allah and break not the covenant,
translations.13:21: And those who maintain all the proper relationships that Allah has commanded to be maintained and fear their Lord and dread a bad account of their own deeds.
translations.13:22: And those who exercise patience seeking the pleasure of their Lord, establish regular prayer, spend out of what We have given them secretly and openly, and repel evil with good. For them is the ultimate/best abode.
translations.13:23: The gardens of perpetual abode which they will enter along with those who had done good deeds from among their parents and their spouses and their offspring. And the angels will enter in upon them from every gate, saying,
translations.13:24: “Peace on you because you exercised patience. How excellent, then, is the ultimate/best abode!”
translations.13:25: And those that break the pledge with Allah after affirming it, sever ties that Allah has ordered to be honoured and spread corruption in the land – for them there is the curse and the wicked abode.
translations.13:26: Allah provides – in abundance or in short measure – for whom He wills. And they rejoice the life of this world, and the life of this world is nothing but a passing provision in the Hereafter.
translations.13:27: And those who suppress the Truth say, “Why is not a sign sent down upon him from his Lord?” Say, “Allah does indeed let him go astray whom He wills. And He guides to Himself those who turn to Him.”
translations.13:28: Those who believe and whose hearts find satisfaction in the remembrance of Allah! Surely, in Allah's remembrance hearts do find satisfaction.
translations.13:29: Those that believe and do good deeds – for them, a state of happiness and a good place to return to.
translations.13:30: Just as We had sent Messengers to their peoples before, We have sent you to these people now, so that you recite to them what We have revealed to you. And they have been suppressing their belief in the Gracious Being! Say, “He is my Lord. There is no god but Him. In Him do I trust and to Him is my return.”
translations.13:31: And the suppressors of the Truth like to think that they would believe if only the mountains were moved by the Qur’aan, or the earth was cut asunder thereby, or the dead were made to speak! But it is Allah Who can do anything and everything. Have the believers then become despaired of the Truth that had Allah so willed, He could have guided all mankind!? And disaster shall not cease to afflict those that suppress the Truth because of what they do, or to strike near their homes, until what Allah has promised comes to pass. And, indeed, Allah fails not to keep the appointment.
translations.13:32: And Messengers that came before you did get mocked at. And I gave those that suppressed the Truth a long rope, and then seized them. And what a punishment it was!
translations.13:33: And they worship others besides Allah – the One Who is ever alert over what everybody does and accordingly deserves! Say, “Name them!” Or, do they tell Him something on the earth of which He is not aware? Or, is it just a show that they make with the words they utter? Nay, the intrigue indulged in by the suppressors of the Truth is made to look pleasing to them and they are held back from the Right Path. And him whom Allah has led astray, no one can guide.
translations.13:34: For them a punishment in the life of this world. And the punishment of the Hereafter is certainly harder. And, there is none to defend them against Allah.
translations.13:35: Some of the things provided in the Garden promised to those who fear Allah: rivers flowing underneath, its fruits and shade everlasting. This is what those who fear Allah shall get in the end. And those who suppress the Truth shall end up in the Fire.
translations.13:36: And those to whom We have given the Book rejoice in that which has been revealed to you. And among groups of people there are some who deny a part of it. Say, “I am commanded that I should worship only Allah and none else. Him I pray to and to Him I return.”
translations.13:37: And thus have We revealed it as the divine code of conduct for humanity in Arabic . And if you follow their desires after what has come to you of knowledge, you shall have neither any wali nor anyone to defend you against Allah.
translations.13:38: And We had certainly sent Messengers before you and gave them wives and children. And no Messenger could bring a sign save by Allah's leave. For every age there is a Book decreed.
translations.13:39: Allah obliterates or preserves what He wills. And with Him is the Master Copy of the (or the Original) Book.
translations.13:40: And whether We let you see part of what We promise them, or We cause you to die before that, your responsibility is only the delivery of the divine Message, and it is for Us to call them to account.
translations.13:41: Do they not see that We are closing in on them from all sides? And Allah judges; His judgment is irrevocable. And He is swift to keeping accounts.
translations.13:42: And those before them did make plans, but all planning is Allah's. He knows what everyone earns, and those who suppress the Truth shall come to know for whom the Ultimate Abode is.
translations.13:43: And those who suppress the Truth say, “You are not a Messenger of Allah.” Say, “Allah – and whoever has knowledge of the Book – is a sufficient witness between me and you.”
translations.14:1: Alif Lam Ra. A Book that We have revealed to you to bring out mankind, by their Lord's permission, from darknesses towards light – towards the way of the Omnipotent, the Praised One,
translations.14:2: Allah is He to Whom belongs whatever there is in the heavens and the earth. And woe, from severe punishment, unto those who suppress the Truth.
translations.14:3: Those who love the life of this world more than the Hereafter and turn people away from Allah's Path and seek perversity in it. They are in deep delusion.
translations.14:4: And We did not send any Messenger with Our Message but in the language of his people, so that he might narrate it to them clearly. Allah then leaves whom He wills to stray away from His Path and He guides whom He wills to His Path. And He is the Omnipotent, the Wise.
translations.14:5: And We did certainly send Moses with Our signs, “Bring out your people from the darknesses towards light and remind them of the annals of Allah. Therein indeed are signs for everyone who is patient and grateful.”
translations.14:6: And when Moses said to his people, “Remember Allah's favour upon you when He delivered you from Pharaoh's people who had subjected you to severe persecution and killed your sons and spared your women. And in this there was a great test from your Lord.”
translations.14:7: And when your Lord declared, “If you are grateful, I will certainly give you more. And if you are ungrateful, My punishment is indeed severe.”
translations.14:8: And Moses said, “Even if you are ungrateful – you and those on earth all together – Allah certainly indeed can do without you being grateful to Him. HE is Self-sufficient, Praiseworthy.”
translations.14:9: Have you not heard of those before you – of the people of Noah and AAad and Thamood, and those after them? None knows them but Allah. Their Messengers came to them with clear signs. And they thrust their hands into their mouths in astonishment while witnessing those signs, but said, “We do not believe in the Message that you are sent with, and we are indeed utterly in doubt about what you invite us to.”
translations.14:10: Their Messengers said, “Is there any doubt about Allah, the Maker of the heavens and the earth? He invites you to Him so that He may forgive you your sins and give you time till an appointed term to redeem yourselves.” They said, “You are but mortals like us. You wish to turn us away from what our fathers worshipped. Bring to us then some clear evidence for this.”
translations.14:11: Their Messengers said to them, “We are but mortals like you, but Allah bestows His favours upon whom He wills of His worshippers. And we cannot bring you any evidence except by Allah's permission. And in Allah then the believers should have trust.”
translations.14:12: “And what reason have we that we should have no trust in Allah, when, surely, He has guided us to our paths? And we shall certainly bear your persecution with patience. And those that trust do trust in Allah.”
translations.14:13: And those who suppressed the Truth told their Messengers, “We shall drive you out of our land, or you shall come back into our lifestyle.” Their Lord then revealed to them, “We will destroy the wicked people.”
translations.14:14: “And We shall certainly settle you in the land after them. That is for him who fears standing in My presence and who fears My warnings.”
translations.14:15: And they sought victory and every obstinate tyrant was frustrated.
translations.14:16: Behind him [every obstinate tyrant] is Hell waiting to quench his thirst with fetid water.
translations.14:17: He will sip at it and it will not be easy for him to swallow it. And death will come to him from every quarter, but he shall not die. And further severe punishment shall await just behind him.
translations.14:18: Deeds of those who suppress the Truth about their Lord are like ashes on which the wind blows hard on a stormy day. They shall have no power over any thing they earn. That is far too grievous a loss of way.
translations.14:19: Do you not see that Allah created the heavens and the earth in Truth? If He wills He will take you off and bring a new creation.
translations.14:20: And that is not at all difficult for Allah!
translations.14:21: And they shall all present themselves before Allah. Then the weak shall say to those who were proud, “We had indeed been your followers. Would you then withstand anything of Allah’s punishment from us?” They would say, “If Allah had guided us, we too would have guided you. It is immaterial to us now whether we bewail or be patient. There is no place for us to escape to.”
translations.14:22: And the Satan shall say after the Judgment is pronounced, “Allah did indeed give you the true promise. And I gave you promises but failed to keep them. And I had no authority over you except to call you to me, and you obeyed me! So, blame me not, but blame yourselves. I cannot be of any help to you now, nor can you be of any help to me. I do indeed deny that you had worshipped me, besides Allah, before.” For the wicked people there shall indeed be a painful punishment.
translations.14:23: And those who believe and do good deeds shall be admitted to gardens beneath which rivers flow. They shall stay eternally therein by their Lord's leave. Their greeting therein shall be, “Peace.”
translations.14:24: Don’t you see how Allah exemplifies a good word as a good tree with firm roots and with its branches spread out in the sky.
translations.14:25: Yielding its fruits every season by its Lord’s leave? And Allah gives examples for the benefit of mankind so that they may ponder and remember.
translations.14:26: And the example of a bad word is a bad tree which can easily be uprooted from the earth's surface; it has no stability.
translations.14:27: Allah establishes, in the life of this world and in the Hereafter, those who believe in the firm Testament . And Allah leads the wicked persons astray, and Allah does what He wills.
translations.14:28: Have you not seen those who have changed Allah's favour by being ungrateful, and made their people eligible for the abode of ruin?
translations.14:29: Hell – they shall burn therein. And a very bad place it is to settle in!
translations.14:30: And they set up rivals to Allah that they may lead people away from His Path. Say, “Enjoy for now! Your destination then shall indeed be to the Fire.”
translations.14:31: Tell My subjects who believe that they should establish regular prayer and spend out of what We have given them, secretly and openly, before the coming of the Day in which there shall be no trading nor any friendly give-and-take.
translations.14:32: Allah is He Who created the heavens and the earth and sent down water from the sky. Then, with it, He brought forth fruits as sustenance for you. And He made the ship subservient to you, so that it sails on the sea by His command. And He made the rivers serve your needs.
translations.14:33: And He made the sun and the moon – both pursuing their respective courses – serve your needs. And He made the night and the day serve you.
translations.14:34: And He gives you of all that you ask Him. And if you would like to know the number of times Allah favours you, you will not be able to count. Man is indeed very wicked, thankless.
translations.14:35: And when Abraham said, “My Lord! Make this a city of peace. And save me and my sons from worshipping idols.”
translations.14:36: “My Lord! They have indeed led many of mankind astray. The one, then, that follows me, is indeed of me. And as for the one that disobeys me, You indeed arc Forgiving, Merciful.”
translations.14:37: “O our Lord! I have indeed inhabited some of my offspring in an uncultivable valley near Your Sacred House, our Lord, that they may establish regular prayer there. Hence make some people come and settle near them and provide them with fruits that they may be grateful.”
translations.14:38: “O our Lord! You do indeed know what we hide and what we do not. And nothing in the earth or in the heaven above is hidden from Allah.”
translations.14:39: “Praise to Allah, Who has given me, in old age, Ishmael and Isaac. My Lord does indeed hear the prayer.”
translations.14:40: “My Lord! Inspire me to establish regular prayer and inspire those from my offspring too. And, O our Lord, accept my prayer!”
translations.14:41: “O our Lord! Pardon me, my parents and the believers on the Day of Accounting!”
translations.14:42: And do not think that Allah is unaware of what the wicked people do. He does but give them respite till the day when the eyes shall get transfixed,
translations.14:43: Hurrying on in fear, their heads upraised – not glancing at themselves – and their hearts vacant.
translations.14:44: And warn people of the Day when the punishment shall come to them. Then those who were wicked will say, “O our Lord! Give us respite for a short term to enable us to respond to Your call and follow the Messengers.” Did you not swear before that you shall have no downfall?
translations.14:45: And you had lived where those, who had transgressed against themselves, lived. And it was clear to you how We had dealt with them. And We had given you examples.
translations.14:46: And they did plan their plan. But their plan is with Allah, though their plan was such that the mountains could come down thereby.
translations.14:47: Think not then that Allah is one to fail in His promise to His Messengers. Allah is indeed Omnipotent and has the power to give a fitting retribution.
translations.14:48: On the Day when the earth shall be changed into a different earth – and the heavens too! And they shall present themselves before Allah, the One, the Almighty.
translations.14:49: And you will see the sinners on that day bound together in chains.
translations.14:50: Their dresses made of tar and the fire covering their faces.
translations.14:51: That Allah may repay everyone for what it earned. Allah is indeed quick in keeping accounts.
translations.14:52: This (Qur’aan) is a sufficient and eloquent exposition of the divine Message for the people that they may be warned thereby, that they may know that He is the One and Only Being worthy of being worshipped, and those endowed with insight may remember it and remind other people about it.
translations.15:1: Alif Lam Ra. Those are Verses of the Book and it is a clear Qur’aan (Reader).
translations.15:2: Those who suppress the Truth shall frequently wish that they had been Muslims (submitters to divine Will).
translations.15:3: Leave them free to eat and enjoy, and let desire distract them. They will soon know.
translations.15:4: And never did We destroy any population, but only when it had reached its known destiny.
translations.15:5: No people can prepone their end nor can they postpone it.
translations.15:6: And they say, “O you to whom the Reminder (Qur’aan) has been revealed! You are indeed mad.”
translations.15:7: “Why do you not bring to us the angels if what you claim is true?”
translations.15:8: We do not send the angels but only when necessary (to destroy wicked persons, for instance), and then they would not be given any respite.
translations.15:9: Indeed, We have sent down the Reminder (Qur’aan) and indeed We are most certainly guardians to it.
translations.15:10: And We did certainly send Messengers, before you, among the earlier generations.
translations.15:11: And never did a Messenger come to them but they mocked him.
translations.15:12: Thus do We make it (making a mockery of a Messenger) enter the hearts of the sinners.
translations.15:13: They believe not in it (the divine Message brought by the Messenger), just as the earlier peoples, that passed away, did not believe (in the divine Messages brought to them by Messengers sent to them).
translations.15:14: And they won’t believe, even if We open to them a gateway into the sky above, and they keep on ascending thereinto.
translations.15:15: They would still only say, “Our sights.are dazed. Nay, we are a people bewitched!”
translations.15:16: And certainly We have made constellations in the skies above and We have made it look beautiful for the beholders.
translations.15:17: And We have guarded it against every accursed Satan.
translations.15:18: Except that a visible flame follows the one that manages to steal a celestial secret.
translations.15:19: And We have spread out the earth and cast mountains into it. And We have caused every appropriate thing to grow in it.
translations.15:20: And We have created in it means of subsistence for you, and for him whom you do not provide for.
translations.15:21: And there is nothing of which We do not have treasures with us. And We do not send it down but in a measured quantity.
translations.15:22: And We send the winds that facilitate pollination , and then send down water from the sky for you to drink. And it is not in your power to store it up.
translations.15:23: And indeed it is We Who give life and cause death. And We are the heirs .
translations.15:24: And We do certainly know those of you who have preceded, and We do certainly know those who would follow.
translations.15:25: And your Lord will indeed gather them together. He is indeed Wise, Knowledgeable.
translations.15:26: And certainly We created man of clay, of moulded mud.
translations.15:27: And the jinn We created before, of radiating fire.
translations.15:28: And your Lord said to the angels, “I am going to create a man of clay, of moulded mud.”
translations.15:29: “So when I give him a proper form and breathe into him of My Rooh , get down to your knees prostrating .”
translations.15:30: So the angels prostrated – all of them together.
translations.15:31: Except Iblees – he refused to be with those who prostrated.
translations.15:32: Allah asked, “O Iblees! What excuses have you that you are not with those who prostrated?”
translations.15:33: Iblees said, “I am not the one to prostrate to a man whom You have created of clay, of moulded mud.”
translations.15:34: Allah said, “Then get out of here! You are indeed damned.”
translations.15:35: “And indeed the curse is on you till the Judgment Day.”
translations.15:36: He said, “My Lord! Then give me respite till the time when they are raised.”
translations.15:37: Allah said, “You are granted the respite”
translations.15:39: He said, “My Lord! As You have made me deviate from the right way, I will certainly keep them enthralled in the earth, and I will certainly cause them all to deviate”
translations.15:40: “Except for those among them who worship you devotedly and exclusively.”
translations.15:41: Allah said, “This is the Path that leads straight to Me.”
translations.15:42: “Indeed, you shall have no power over My subjects, except those who follow you in deviating from the right way.”
translations.15:43: And indeed Hell is the place promised to them all.
translations.15:44: It has seven gates assigned separately to separate sections of them.
translations.15:45: And those who fear Allah shall indeed be in gardens and fountains.
translations.15:46: They shall enter there in peace and security.
translations.15:47: And We will clean their hearts of any rancour. They shall be on couches face to face as brethren. 15:48 They shall not have to suffer any fatigue therein, nor shall they be ever ejected from it.
translations.15:49: Inform my subjects that I am the Forgiver, the Merciful. 15:50 And that My punishment is the painful one.
translations.15:51: And tell them about Abraham’s guests.
translations.15:52: When they came to him and greeted, “Peace!” He said, “We are indeed apprehensive of you.”
translations.15:53: They said, “Be not afraid! We do indeed give you the good news of a learned son.”
translations.15:54: He said, “Do you give me the good news when old age has come upon me? What kind of good news is this that you give me?”
translations.15:55: They said, “There is truth in the good news We give you! So be not of those who despair.
translations.15:56: He said, “And who would despair of the Mercy of his Lord but those who have gone astray?”
translations.15:57: He said, “What is your mission then, O Messengers?”
translations.15:58: They said, “We have indeed been sent towards a sinning people.”
translations.15:59: “Excepting Lot's family, all of whom We certainly will save.”
translations.15:60: “But not his wife. We ordained that she shall indeed be of those who remain behind.”
translations.15:61: So when the Messengers came to Lot's family,
translations.15:63: They said, “But we have come to you with that about which they had doubts.” 15:64 “And we have come to you with the authority, and what we say is most certainly the truth.”
translations.15:65: “So leave this place with members of your family in a part of the night and you yourself follow their rear. And let not any one of you turn around. And proceed whither you are commanded.”
translations.15:66: And We declared to him that decree, that the roots of these (people left behind) shall be cut off by the morning.
translations.15:67: And the people of the town came to him rejoicing.
translations.15:68: He said, “These are my guests! Disgrace me not.”
translations.15:69: “And fear Allah and do not put me to shame.” 15:70 They said, “Have we not prohibited you from meddling with other people’s matters?”
translations.15:71: He said, “Here are my daughters, if you have to do it. ”
translations.15:72: By your life! They were wandering blindly in their intoxication.
translations.15:73: Then, at sunrise, the terribly rumbling sound struck them.
translations.15:74: We turned the place upside down, and rained stones of baked clay upon the people.
translations.15:75: In that indeed are signs for those with insight.
translations.15:76: And indeed it is located on an established road.
translations.15:77: Indeed there is a sign in that for the believers.
translations.15:78: And the dwellers of the wood too were most wicked.
translations.15:79: So We avenged them, and they are both , indeed, on an open road.
translations.15:80: And the dwellers of the Rock did certainly reject the Messengers.
translations.15:81: And We gave them Our Verses/signs, but they turned away from them.
translations.15:82: And they were wont to hew houses in the mountains for security.
translations.15:83: So the terribly rumbling sound seized them in the morning.
translations.15:84: And what they earned availed them not.
translations.15:85: And We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them, but by due authority. And the Hour is most surely going to come. So fogive things – those that are forgivable.
translations.15:86: Your Lord is indeed the Knowledgeable Creator of all things.
translations.15:87: And certainly We have given you seven of the repeated Verses and the Glorious Qur’aan.
translations.15:88: Pine not for that which We have given some of them to enjoy, nor grieve for them. And be kind to the believers.
translations.15:89: And say, “I am indeed the plain warner.”
translations.15:90: It is as though what We have sent down is on those who make divisions therein.
translations.15:91: Those that tear the Qur’aan into shreds.
translations.15:92: And, by your Lord, We shall certainly question them all
translations.15:94: Declare then openly what you are commanded with and turn away from the polytheists.
translations.15:95: We shall certainly provide you sufficient protection against those who scoff.
translations.15:96: Those who worship others besides Allah. They shall soon know their folly!
translations.15:97: And We do know that your (Prophet’s) heart is distressed at what they say.
translations.15:98: Hymn then the praise of your Lord and be of those who prostrate to Him.
translations.15:99: And worship your Lord until there comes to you that certain composure.
translations.16:1: Allah's command is already issued! So, hasten it not. Glorified and highly exalted is He above what they worship besides Him.
translations.16:2: He sends down the angels, on whom He pleases of His subjects, with the inspiration of His command: “Give the warning that there is no god but Me, and so fear Me.”
translations.16:3: He has created the heavens and the earth with the Truth and due Authority. He is far too high and exalted above what they worship besides Him.
translations.16:4: He (Allah) created man from tiny fertilized ovum. And, lo, he has become an open adversary!
translations.16:5: And He created the cattle. You get from them warm clothing and other benefits. And from them you get meat to eat.
translations.16:6: And you feel good when you drive them back home, and when you send them out to pasture.
translations.16:7: And they carry your heavy loads to lands you could not reach but with much difficulty. Your Lord is indeed Kind, Merciful.
translations.16:8: And horses, mules and asses for you to ride upon and as assets, and He creates what you do not know.
translations.16:9: And upon Allah is the determination of the Path and the deviations from it. And if He had so willed He would have certainly guided you all.
translations.16:10: He it is Who sends down water from the sky. You drink thereof, and there from you get the shrubbery upon which you let your cattle graze.
translations.16:11: He causes the green cultivated fields, the olives, the palm trees, the grapes and all kinds of fruits to grow for you thereby. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who think.
translations.16:12: And He has made the night, the day, the sun and the moon to work for you. And the stars are made subservient by His command. There are indeed signs in that for a people who ponder.
translations.16:13: And what He has created on the earth for you are of different hues and colours. There is a sign in that for a people who reflect.
translations.16:14: And He it is Who has made the sea subservient so that you may eat fresh flesh from it and bring out of it jewels you wear. And you see the ships cut through it so that you might seek His bounty and be thankful.
translations.16:15: And He has cast firm anchors [mountains] in the earth lest it should shake with you. And rivers and roads, that you may find your way.
translations.16:16: And they find their way by landmarks and the stars.
translations.16:17: Is He then Who creates like the one who does not? Do you not then reflect?
translations.16:18: And if you would count Allah's favours, you won’t be able to do it. Allah is certainly indeed Forgiving, Merciful.
translations.16:19: And Allah knows what you conceal and what you do openly.
translations.16:20: And those, whom they pray to besides Allah, have not created anything; they are themselves created!
translations.16:21: They are dead – not living – and they know not when they shall be raised.
translations.16:22: The object of your worship is Allah, the One and Only! And those, who do not believe in the Hereafter, are defiant in their hearts, and arrogant.
translations.16:23: Allah does indeed, without doubt, know what they hide and what they declare. He does not, indeed, love those who are arrogant.
translations.16:24: And when they are asked, “What is it that your Lord has sent down?” They say, “Stories of the ancients.”
translations.16:25: By saying so they are going to bear not only their own entire burdens on the day of Resurrection, but also of the burdens of those whom they led astray with misinformation. Is it not too bad what they bear?
translations.16:26: Those before them plotted. But Allah struck their edifice at its foundations, so that the roof fell down on them from above them. And the punishment came to them from whence they knew not.
translations.16:27: Then on the Resurrection Day He will disgrace them and ask, “Where are they whom you worshipped besides Me, and for whose sake you caused discord?” Those who are endowed with knowledge will say, “Indeed, the disgrace and the evil, this day, shall be upon those who had suppressed the Truth.”
translations.16:28: Those that the angels caused to die while they indulged in deeds, which were ultimately injurious to their own selves. Then they would submit, “We didn’t do anything bad.” Aye! Allah does indeed know what you did.
translations.16:29: Enter then the gates of Hell, to be there forever! And the dwelling place of the arrogant people is certainly bad.
translations.16:30: And those who fear Allah are asked, “What is it that your Lord has sent down?” They say, “That which is good.” There is goodness for those who do good deeds in this world. And certainly, the abode of the Hereafter is better. And excellent certainly is the abode of those who fear Allah.
translations.16:31: They shall enter gardens of perpetuity, having rivers flowing beneath them. They shall have, in them, what they please. Thus, does Allah reward those who fear Him.
translations.16:32: Those that the angels caused to die while they did good deeds, the angels saying to them, “Peace upon you! Enter the garden as a reward for what you did.”
translations.16:33: They do not wait for anything but that the angels should come to them or that the Commandment of your Lord should come to pass. That is what those before them did. And Allah oppressed them not, but they oppressed themselves.
translations.16:34: So the bad things they did shall afflict them and the things they mocked at shall besiege them.
translations.16:35: And they who worship others besides Allah say, “Had Allah so willed, we would not have worshipped anything besides Him. Nor would our fathers do it. And we would not have prohibited anything without His Command.” That is what those before them did. Are the Messengers then responsible for anything but the plain and clear conveyance of the divine Message?
translations.16:36: And certainly We raised in every community a Messenger saying, “Worship Allah and shun whatever is worshipped other than Allah.” So, there were some, among them, whom Allah guided and there were others who were destined to be misguided. Travel in the land then and see what the end of those who rejected the divine Message was.
translations.16:37: Even if you are anxious for their guidance, Allah does not indeed guide him who willfully goes astray. And there shall be none to help them.
translations.16:38: And they swear by Allah most solemnly that Allah will not raise anyone who dies. Yea! It is a promise binding on Him, but most people know it not.
translations.16:39: Allah will raise the dead so that He might make matters, in which they differ, clear to them, and that those who suppress the Truth might know that they were liars.
translations.16:40: When We intend a thing, We just say “Be” and it is.
translations.16:41: And those who migrate for Allah's sake after they are oppressed, We will certainly resettle them in a good position in this world. And the reward of the Hereafter is certainly much greater, if they but know.
translations.16:42: Those that are patient and have trust in their Lord.
translations.16:43: And We did not send before you any but men to whom We revealed our Verses/signs. Ask the people of the divine Reminder/Book if you do not know.
translations.16:44: We had sent only men, to the earlier people, with clear signs/evidences and scriptures. And We have sent down to you the Reminder (the Qur’aan) that you may make clear to mankind what has been sent down for them, and that they may think about it. 16:45 Do they then who plan evil deeds feel confident that Allah will not cause the earth to swallow them, or that punishment may not overtake them from whence they know not?
translations.16:46: Or that He may not seize them while they are on the move, and so they cannot escape?
translations.16:47: Or that He may not seize them with fear and/or slow destruction? And your Lord is indeed Kind, Merciful.
translations.16:48: Do they not see that anything, which Allah has created, casts its shadow right and left in humble prostration to Allah?
translations.16:49: And to Allah does prostrate any creature that is in the heavens and that is in the earth, and the angels too do protrate. And they do not show pride.
translations.16:50: They fear their Lord over them. And they do what they are commanded.
translations.16:51: And Allah tells human beings not to take two gods. He is Allah, the One and Only. “So, Me alone should you hold in awe.”
translations.16:52: And whatever is in the heavens and the earth is His, and the way of life ought to be for Him ever. Will you then fear any being other than Allah?
translations.16:53: And the good things, you have, are all from Allah. Then when anything bad afflicts you, to Him do you complain loudly!
translations.16:54: And yet when He removes the hardship from you, lo, some of you worship others besides their Lord!
translations.16:55: And so they become ungrateful for what We have given them. Enjoy then for now! You shall soon come to know your real destiny.
translations.16:56: And they set apart a portion of what We have given them for those of whom they know nothing . By Allah, you shall most certainly be questioned about that which you have been concocting!
translations.16:57: And they arbitrarily attribute daughters (angels) to Allah. HE is too glorious to have any such creaturely attribute. And, for themselves, they would have what they desire (sons)!
translations.16:58: And when news of a daughter being born is given to any of them, his face becomes darkened with suppressed anger.
translations.16:59: He hides himself from the people because of the stigma of the news given to him. Shall he keep it despite the disgrace, or bury it in the dust? Verily, their judgment is vicious.
translations.16:60: An example of evil applies to those who believe not in the Hereafter. And the best/loftiest example applies to Allah. And He is the Omnipotent, the Wise.
translations.16:61: And had Allah seized mankind for their unjust acts, He would not have left, on the earth, even a single creature. But He gives them a definite time limit. And when their time comes, they shall not be able to postpone it for a moment nor prepone it. 16:62 And they assign to Allah what they hate for themselves. And their tongues philosophically cover up the lie to say that for them there shall be nothing but the good. There is no doubt that for them there shall be the Fire and that they shall be hastened thereto.
translations.16:63: By Allah, We did certainly send Messengers to peoples that existed before you. Then the Satan made their deeds seem good to them. And he became their wali of the day (period). And they shall have a painful punishment.
translations.16:64: And We have not sent down the Book to you but to make clear to them what they differ in, and as Guidance and Mercy for people who believe.
translations.16:65: And Allah it is Who sends down water from the sky and therewith gives life to the earth after its death. There is indeed a sign in that for people who listen.
translations.16:66: And, indeed, in the cattle there is a lesson for you. We give you pure, potable drink of milk out of what they consume in their bellies and drawn from excreta and blood.
translations.16:67: And from the fruits of the palm-tree and the vine, you get intoxication as well as good food. There is indeed a sign in that for a people who ponder.
translations.16:68: And your Lord inspired the bee to “have hives in the mountains, in the trees and in the buildings.”
translations.16:69: “And then eat of all the fruits and tread the paths of your Lord submissively.” A beverage of various colours comes forth from within it, wherein there is curative value for mankind. There is indeed a sign in that for people who reflect.
translations.16:70: And Allah it is Who has created you. It is He then Who causes you to die. And among you there may be someone who, after having acquired knowledge, is reduced to such a decrepit state of life that he knows nothing. Allah is indeed Knowledgeable, Powerful.
translations.16:71: And it is Allah Who has given some of you more/better provisions than to others. Would then those who are thus favoured give away their provisions to their slaves so that they become equal therein? Do they then deny this privilege that Allah has given them?
translations.16:72: And it is Allah Who has made spouses for you from among yourselves, and through your spouses He has given you sons, daughters and their further families. And He has provided wholesome food for you. Do they believe then in the falsehood and suppress the fact that it is Allah Who has given them the good things they enjoy?
translations.16:73: And they worship, besides Allah, those that possess nothing, in the heavens and the earth, wherewith to sustain their worshippers. Nor have those any power to possess anything.
translations.16:74: So liken not anything to Allah! Indeed, Allah knows, and you do not.
translations.16:75: Allah gives an example: a slave – property of another person – who has no power over anything, and, on the other hand, a free man whom We have given a good provision from Ourselves and he spends from it secretly and openly. Are the two alike? To Allah is due all praise! But, most of them know not.
translations.16:76: And Allah gives another example, of two men, one of whom is dumb, unable to do anything, and a burden on his master. He is not good at whatever task the master gives him. Can he be held equal to him who enjoins what is just, and is himself on the right path?
translations.16:77: And with Allah is the knowledge of the unseen/secrets of the heavens and the earth. And the happening of the Hour is but as a momentary sight or consuming even less time. Allah indeed can do anything.
translations.16:78: And it is Allah Who brought you out from the wombs of your mothers, knowing nothing. And He gave you hearing and sight and intellect that you may be grateful.
translations.16:79: See they not how the birds are enabled to be in mid-air above? None keeps them there but Allah! Indeed, there are signs in that for people who believe.
translations.16:80: And Allah has facilitated for you rest, security and solace in your houses. And He has facilitated for you tents of the skins of cattle which you find light to carry when you travel and when you stay out. And of the animal wool, fur and hair, He has given you assets and possessions for a time.
translations.16:81: And Allah has made for you shelters from what He has created, and places of retreat in the mountains. And He has provided for garments for you to protect you from the heat and for armour to protect you in your armed conflicts. Thus does He complete His favour upon you in order that you submit to Him.
translations.16:82: And if, even then, they turn away, the responsibility on you (Prophet Muhammad) is only to deliver Allah’s Message clearly to the people.
translations.16:83: They are aware of Allah’s favour, yet they deny it! And most of them are those who suppress the Truth.
translations.16:84: And One Day We will raise a witness out of every community. Then shall no leeway be given to those who suppress the Truth, nor shall they be allowed to solicit favours.
translations.16:85: And when those who are wicked experience the punishment, its severity on them shall not be decreased, nor shall they be given any respite.
translations.16:86: And when those who worship others besides Allah see those others, they shall say, “Our Lord! These are our other gods whom we prayed to besides You.” But they will retort, “Certainly indeed you are liars!”
translations.16:87: And they shall tender submission to Allah on that day. And what they used to concoct shall depart from them.
translations.16:88: For those who suppress the Truth and cause hindrance in Allah's Path, We will add punishment over punishment because they have been spreading corruption.
translations.16:89: And One Day We will raise in every people a witness against them from among themselves and bring you as a witness against these. And We have revealed to you the Book explaining everything clearly, and as a guidance, mercy and good news for those who submit.
translations.16:90: Allah does indeed enjoin justice, kindness and giving things to near and dear ones. And He forbids obscenity, abomination, and rebellion. He admonishes you that you may remember and take heed.
translations.16:91: And fulfill Allah’s covenant when you make one. And break not oaths after affirming them; for, then, you have indeed made Allah stand surety for you. Allah does indeed know what you do.
translations.16:92: And be not like the woman who disintegrates her well-spun yarn into fibres. You take your oaths as means of interference in one another’s affairs so that one community gets bigger than another. Allah has not but put you on trial by this; and He will certainly make clear to you, on the Resurrection Day, that, about which you differed.
translations.16:93: And if Allah had so willed, He would certainly have made you a single community. But He causes whom He wills to go astray and guides whom He wills to go on the Right Path. And you will certainly be questioned as to what you did.
translations.16:94: And take not your oaths as means of interference in one another’s affairs, lest a footstep should stumble after it is firmly taken and you should taste adversity because you hindered someone from Allah's Path. And grievous punishment should be your lot.
translations.16:95: And trade not covenant with Allah, for petty gains. That which is with Allah is certainly better for you, if you but knew.
translations.16:96: What is with you gets exhausted and what is with Allah endures. And We will certainly give to those who are patient their reward for the good things they did.
translations.16:97: Whoever, male or female, does good work, and he/she is a believer, We will certainly make him/her live a good and clean life, and We will certainly give them their reward for the good things they did.
translations.16:98: And when you recite the Qur’aan, seek refuge with Allah from the accursed Satan.
translations.16:99: Indeed he (Satan) has no authority over those who believe and have trust in their Lord.
translations.16:100: His authority is only over those who befriend him and over those who worship him besides Allah. 16:101 And when We change one Message for another – and Allah knows best what He reveals – they say, “You are only a forger.” Nay, most of them know not.
translations.16:102: Say, “The Holy Spirit has authoritatively revealed it (the Qur’aan) as from your Lord, in order that it may stabilise those who believe and give guidance and good news for those who submit.”
translations.16:103: And We are certainly aware that they say, “A certain man it is who teaches him.” The person they are alluding to speaks a foreign tongue, and this is clear Arabic tongue!
translations.16:104: Allah does indeed not guide those who do not believe in His Messages. And they shall have a painful punishment.
translations.16:105: Those that believe not in Allah’s Messages, it is they who concoct the lie. And those are the liars.
translations.16:106: Allah’s Wrath is on people who deny Allah after having believed in Him, not on those compelled while their hearts are at rest with faith, but on those that open their minds to suppression of the Truth. And they shall have the most severe punishment.
translations.16:107: That is because they love this worldly life more than the Hereafter, and because Allah does not guide people who suppress the Truth.
translations.16:108: These are the people on whose hearts, hearing and eyes Allah has set a seal. And they take no heed!
translations.16:109: No doubt, in the Hereafter, they will be the doomed ones.
translations.16:110: And then, on the other hand, your Lord is indeed Forgiving and Merciful on those who migrate after they are persecuted, then struggle hard and are patient.
translations.16:111: On that Day every one shall come, pleading for one’s own self. And every one shall be paid one’s full dues, and they shall not be wronged.
translations.16:112: And Allah gives an example of a village/town, safe and secure, to which its means of subsistence came in abundance from every quarter. Then it became ungrateful to Allah's favours. And Allah made it wear the garb of hunger and fear because of what they wrought.
translations.16:113: And, of course, a Messenger from among them had come to them, but they rejected him. So disaster struck them because of their wickedness.
translations.16:114: Eat then of what Allah has given you, lawful and wholesome. And be grateful for Allah's favours, if Him it is that you worship!
translations.16:115: HE has forbidden you only carrion (what dies of itself), blood, flesh of swine and that over which any name, other than that of Allah, has been invoked. But if anyone is driven by necessity – and not desire – and does not exceed limits, then Allah is indeed Forgiving, Merciful.
translations.16:116: And describe not anything falsely, with your tongues, as being lawful or unlawful so as to concoct a lie and ascribe it to Allah. Indeed, those who concoct a lie and ascribe it to Allah shall not prosper.
translations.16:117: They will enjoy a little and then have a painful punishment.
translations.16:118: And for those who were Jews, We prohibited what We have related to you already. . And We did them no wrong, but they wronged themselves
translations.16:119: And your Lord is indeed Forgiving and Merciful to those who do an evil act in ignorance, then turn in repentance after that and make amends.
translations.16:120: Abraham was indeed an institution by himself, firmly committed to Allah. And he was never a polytheist.
translations.16:121: He was ever grateful for Allah’s favours. HE chose him and guided him to the Straight Path.
translations.16:122: And We gave him a good life in this world, and, in the next, he will indeed be among the good, righteous people.
translations.16:123: We then instructed you to follow Abraham’s lifestyle steadfastly. And he was not of the polytheists.
translations.16:124: The Sabbath was ordained only for those who differed about it, and your Lord indeed will judge between them on the Resurrection Day concerning that about which they differed.
translations.16:125: Call people to the Path of your Lord with wisdom and good counsel, and argue with them in the best possible manner. Your Lord does indeed know who go astray from His path, and who follow it.
translations.16:126: And if you retaliate, then retaliate with the like of that with which you were afflicted. But if you are patient, it will certainly be better for those who are patient.
translations.16:127: And be patient and your patience is not but by Allah’s Grace. And grieve not for them, and do not distress yourself at what they plot. .
translations.16:128: Allah is indeed with those who fear Him and those who do good deeds
translations.17:1: Glorified He is Who took His devotee on a night journey from the Sacred Place of Worship (at Makkah) to the Distant Place of Worship , the precincts of which We have blessed, so that We may show to him some of Our signs. It is He indeed Who hears, sees, everything.
translations.17:2: And We gave Moses the Book and made it a guidance for the Children of Israel, “Entrust none but Me for management of your affairs!”
translations.17:3: They were offspring of those whom We bore with Noah on the Ark. He was indeed a grateful subject.
translations.17:4: And We had decreed, in the Book, to the Children of Israel, “You will surely make mischief on the earth twice, and you will surely rise to great heights of haughtiness.”
translations.17:5: So when the first of the two prophecies came to pass, We let loose over you Our subjects of mighty strength; and they ravaged through the houses. And it was a prophecy accomplished.
translations.17:6: Then We gave you back the turn to prevail against them and aided you with wealth and children and made you a numerous predominant force.
translations.17:7: If you do good deeds, you do it for your own good. And if you do bad deeds, you do it to your own detriment. So, when the second prophecy would come so that they (your enemies) disgrace you and enter the Place of Worship as they had entered it the first time, and that they might destroy utterly whatever they lay their hands on.
translations.17:8: It may be that your Lord will have mercy on you. And if you revert to disobedience, We too will revert to punishing you. And We have made Hell a prison for those who suppress the Truth.
translations.17:9: This Qur’aan does indeed guide to that which is straight and stable. And it gives the good news to the believers who do good work that they shall have a great reward.
translations.17:10: And the Qur’aan warns those who do not believe in the Hereafter that We have prepared a painful punishment for them.
translations.17:11: And man prays for evil as he ought to pray for good. And man is ever hasty.
translations.17:12: And We have made the night and the day as two signs. We efface the sign of the night and make the sign of the day clear, so that you may seek favour from your Lord, know the calendar and keep accounts. And We have explained everything in detail.
translations.17:13: And We have tied every man's actions to his neck. And We will bring out for him on the Resurrection Day a record thrown wide open.
translations.17:14: “Read your record! You yourself are sufficient as an auditor against you this day.”
translations.17:15: One that goes on the right path does so for one’s own self. And one that goes astray does so to one’s own detriment. And no bearer of a burden bears the burden of another, nor do We punish until We send a Messenger.
translations.17:16: And when We wish to destroy a town, We send Our commandment to its wealthy people who lead easy lives, but they defiantly disobey! So, the divine Word takes effect against that town, and We deal with it destructively.
translations.17:17: And many a generation, after Noah, did We destroy! And your Lord has a sufficient knowledge of and is a sufficient Witness to His subjects' faults/mistakes/sins.
translations.17:18: As for one who desires instant gains in this present life, We do hasten therein, to whom We will, what We please. Then We make the Hell burn, to disgrace and reject that one.
translations.17:19: And as for one who desires the Hereafter and strives for it as one ought to strive and is a believer, the strivings of such people are duly accepted.
translations.17:20: We do aid all – these (mentioned in Verse 19 above) as well as those (mentioned in Verse 18 above) – out of things gifted by your Lord. And the gifts (like air and water) of your Lord are not restricted.
translations.17:21: See how We have favoured some of them over others. And, of course, the Hereafter is much superior in ranks and favours. 17:22 Worship none but Allah lest you remain disgraced, forsaken.
translations.17:23: And your Lord has commanded that you shall worship none but Him, and that you shall be good to your parents. If either or both reach old age while with you, you shall not utter even a word of the slightest annoyance or reproach to them. And speak to them kindly.
translations.17:24: And be submissively and compassionately protective of them, and pray, ‘O my Lord! Have mercy on them as they had brought me up during my childhood.’
translations.17:25: Your Lord knows what is in your minds. If you are good, then He does indeed forgive those who often turn to Him in penitence.
translations.17:26: And give to the near of kin his/her due, and to the needy and the wayfarer their dues. And be not a spendthrift.
translations.17:27: Indeed, the spendthrifts are brothers of the satanic people and the Satan is ever ungrateful to his Lord.
translations.17:28: And if you are not able to help them out and you are yourself seeking mercy from your Lord, speak to them a kind word.
translations.17:29: And do not make your hand tied to your neck nor extend it to its utmost limit, lest you should be left blamed and impoverished.
translations.17:30: Your Lord does indeed enlarge means of subsistence for those He wills and restrict for those He wills. He does indeed have full knowledge of and does constantly keep watch on His creatures that always ought to obey Him.
translations.17:31: And kill not your children for fear of poverty. We give them sustenance as We give it to you. Killing them is indeed a great wrong.
translations.17:32: And go not near fornication/adultery! It is indeed an abomination and an evil path.
translations.17:33: And do not kill any one, whose killing Allah has forbidden, unless duly authorized to do so. And whoever is slain unjustly, We have of course given to his heir power to retaliate. But let him not then exceed just limits in killing. He does indeed deserve help.
translations.17:34: And come not near the property of the orphan except as propriety demands till he attains his maturity. And keep the promise you make. The promise shall indeed be questioned about.
translations.17:35: And give full measure when you give anything by measure, and weigh with a true balance. That is fair and yields a better result in the end.
translations.17:36: And follow not that of which you have no knowledge! The hearing, the sight and the mind shall all be questioned thereon.
translations.17:37: And walk not on earth exultantly, for you cannot tear the earth nor reach the mountains in height.
translations.17:38: All that – the evil thereof – is hateful in the sight of your Lord.
translations.17:39: That is of what your Lord has revealed to you of wisdom. And worship not any god other than Allah lest you should be thrown into Hell, blamed, rejected.
translations.17:40: Has then your Lord chosen sons for you, and taken daughters for Himself from among the angels!? What you say is indeed very grave!
translations.17:41: And We certainly have explained things variously in this Qur’aan that they may take heed. But it adds nothing but aversion to their credit!
translations.17:42: Say, “If, as they say, there were other gods along with Him, then certainly they would have sought a way to the Lord of the Throne.”
translations.17:43: Glorified He is and exalted extremely high above what they say.
translations.17:44: The seven heavens, the earth and whoever or whatever is in them declare His glory. And there is not a thing but glorifies Him with His praise, but their glorification you understand not. He is indeed Considerate, Forgiving.
translations.17:45: And when you recite the Qur’aan, We place between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter a hidden barrier.
translations.17:46: And We have placed a veil on their hearts and deafness in their ears lest they understand it. And when you mention only your Lord (and not their deities other than Allah) in the Qur’aan, they turn their backs in aversion.
translations.17:47: We know best what they listen to when they listen to you, and when they talk secretly among themselves. Then the wicked people say, “You follow none but one who is under a magic spell.”
translations.17:48: See what they liken you to! That is because they have gone astray and cannot find the way.
translations.17:49: And they ask, “When we shall have become bones and dust (after death), shall we even then be raised up as a new creation!? ”
translations.17:50: Say, “Even when you become stones or iron,”
translations.17:51: “Or some other creation that is too hard to be revived to life in your minds!” Then they will ask, “Who will revive us?” Say, “He Who created you the first time.” Then they will shake their heads at you in disbelief and say, “When will it be?” Say, “Maybe it is near. ”
translations.17:52: On that Day of Resurrection, He will call you, and you shall respond uttering His praise. And you will think that you had remained in the grave for only a little while.
translations.17:53: And say to My human creatures, who all ought to obey Me, that they speak that which is most appropriate and proper. Indeed, the Satan sows discord among them. The Satan is indeed an open enemy to man.
translations.17:54: Your Lord knows you. He will have mercy on you, or He will punish you, as He wills. And We have not sent you as the manager of their affairs.
translations.17:55: And your Lord knows those who are in the heavens and the earth. And We have certainly favoured some of the prophets over others. And We gave David the Psalms.
translations.17:56: Say, “Call on whom you want to, besides Him! They cannot remove the distress from you nor can they change it.”
translations.17:57: They themselves – whom they call upon – seek the means of getting closest, among themselves, to their Lord. And they hope for His mercy and fear His punishment. Indeed, the punishment of your Lord is a thing to be afraid of.
translations.17:58: And not a village/town/city but We will destroy it before the Day of Resurrection or punish it with a severe punishment. That stands recorded in the Divine Ordinance.
translations.17:59: And nothing prevented Us from sending miracles except that the ancients rejected them. And We gave Thamood the she-camel – a manifest miracle – but they wronged her. And We do not send miracles but to warn.
translations.17:60: And We did say to you, “Your Lord has indeed kept mankind under His control. And We did not make the vision which We showed you – as also the tree cursed in the Qur’aan – but a trial for mankind. And We warn them. But it only adds to their utter transgression.”
translations.17:61: And when We asked the angels to prostrate to Adam, they prostrated. But Iblees did not. He said, “Shall I prostrate to him whom You have created of dust?”
translations.17:62: He said, “Do you see whom You have honoured above me? If You should give me parole till the Day of Resurrection, I will most certainly keep a tight leash on his progeny but for a few of them.”
translations.17:63: Allah said, “Get out of here! And Hell is the due recompense to you and to all those who, among them, will follow you.”
translations.17:64: “And beguile whom you can of them with your voice, and cover them with your thinking and your behaviour, and be their partner in wealth and children, and feed them with promises.” And the Satan promises them not but to deceive.
translations.17:65: “Indeed, you have no authority over all those whom I have created and who all obey Me. And your Lord is sufficient as a Guardian/Manager of their interests.”
translations.17:66: Your Lord is He Who navigates the ships for you in the sea that you may seek things out of His grace. He is indeed ever Merciful to you.
translations.17:67: And when, while on the sea, distress strikes you, all, but He, whom you pray to, desert you. Then when He brings you back safe to the land, you turn away from Him. And man is ever ungrateful.
translations.17:68: Do you then feel secure that He will not crush you under a landslide or send upon you a violent storm? Then you may not find anyone to save you.
translations.17:69: Or, do you feel secure that He will not take you back into it (sea) another time, then send on you a stormy wind and thus drown you because of your ungratefulness? Then you may not find anyone to help you against Us in the matter.
translations.17:70: And verily We have been liberal to the children of Adam, and We have carried them in the land and the sea, and We have provided them with wholesome good things, and We have preferred them, in granting favours, over most of our creations.
translations.17:71: On the Day of Resurrection, We will call every people with their Imam. Then those shall read their books, every one of whom will be given his book in his right hand. And they shall not be wronged a bit.
translations.17:72: And he, who is blind in this world and has strayed away from the Path, shall also be blind in the Hereafter.
translations.17:73: And, indeed, they wanted to turn you away from that which We have revealed to you, so that you concoct against Us something else. And then they would certainly have taken you as a friend.
translations.17:74: And had We not made you firm, you would almost certainly have inclined to them a little.
translations.17:75: In that case We would certainly have doubled for you the suffering in life and in death. Then you would not have found any one to help you against Us.
translations.17:76: And, indeed, they wanted to make you unsettled in the land to drive you there from. And in that case, they would not have lived there after you but a little.
translations.17:77: This was Our custom about those of Our Messengers whom We had sent before you, and you shall find no change in Our custom.
translations.17:78: Establish ritual prayer during the interval from the time the sun sets till the darkness of the night spreads completely. And recite the Qur’aan at dawn. The recitation at dawn is indeed to be in the presence of a congregation.
translations.17:79: And keep awake part of the night reciting it (Qur’aan). This is an additional thing for you (the Messenger) to do. Maybe your Lord will raise you to a praiseworthy position.
translations.17:80: And say, “My Lord! Grant me a truly sound entry into and a truly sound exit from any course of action I undertake in this world. And My Lord! Grant me Your powerful support in whatever good I do.”
translations.17:81: And say, “The Truth has come, and the falsehood has perished. The falsehood is bound to perish.”
translations.17:82: And We reveal, of the Qur’aan, that which is a healing and a mercy to the believers. And it adds to nothing but doom of the wicked people.
translations.17:83: And when We bestow some favour on man, he keeps himself arrogantly aloof. And when anything bad happens to him, he is in despair.
translations.17:84: Say, “Everyone goes his own way. And your Lord knows who it is that is guided to the right path.”
translations.17:85: And they ask you about the soul. Say, “The soul is one of the commands of my Lord. And you are not given knowledge but a little thereof.”
translations.17:86: And if We so will, We could certainly take away that which We have revealed to you. Then you would not find anyone to guard it for you against Our doing so
translations.17:87: Except for Mercy from your Lord! His favour upon you is indeed abundant.
translations.17:88: Say, “Even if men and jinn should join together and help each other to bring about the like of this Qur’aan, they would not be able to do it.”
translations.17:89: And certainly, in this Qur’aan, We have variously explained every aspect of life for mankind. But most of them did not but reject it ungratefully.
translations.17:90: And they say, “We will not believe in you until you cause a fountain to gush forth from the earth for us.”
translations.17:91: “Or there are for you a garden of date-palms and grape-vines with rivers flowing luxuriantly through them.”
translations.17:92: “Or you cause the heaven to come down upon us in pieces as you think it would, or you bring Allah and the angels before us.”
translations.17:93: “Or you have a house of gold, or you ascend into heaven, and we will not believe in your ascent until you bring down to us a book for us to read.” Say, “Glorified is my Lord! Am I anything but a mortal man sent with a divine Message?”
translations.17:94: And nothing prevented people from believing when the divine Guidance came to them except for what they said, “Has Allah raised a mortal man as His Messenger!?”
translations.17:95: Say, “Had angels been the normal inhabitants on earth, We would certainly have sent down to them from the heaven an angel as divine Messenger.”
translations.17:96: Say, “Allah suffices as a witness between me and you. HE is indeed well aware and minutely observant of all His human creatures.”
translations.17:97: And the one, whom Allah guides, is the one who is guided. And the one, whom He leaves to go astray, you shall not find any awliya for him besides Him. And We will gather them together, on the Day of Resurrection, all lying on their faces – blind, dumb and deaf. Their abode shall be in Hell. We will increase the intensity of the blazing Fire, whenever it abates, for them.
translations.17:98: That is their retribution because they suppressed the truth of Our Messages/Verses/Signs and because they said, “What! When we shall have become bones and dust, shall we then indeed be raised up as a new creation?”
translations.17:99: Do they not see that Allah – Who created the heavens and the earth and appointed an end for them wherein there in no doubt – is able to create their like? But the wicked people do not but suppress the Truth.
translations.17:100: Say, “Even if you were in control of the treasures of the Mercy of my Lord, you would miserly hold on to it for fear of exhausting it. And man is stingy.”
translations.17:101: And We did give Moses nine clear signs. Ask the Children of Israel about them. When he came to them, Pharaoh told him, “I do indeed consider you, O Moses, to be a man bewitched.”
translations.17:102: Moses said, “You do know that none, but the Lord of the heavens and the earth has sent down these as clear evidence. And I do indeed consider you, O Pharaoh, to be a man doomed.”
translations.17:103: Then Pharaoh wanted to get them destabilised in the land. And We drowned him and those with him all together.
translations.17:104: And, afterwards, We said to the Children of Israel, “Settle down in the land. Then when the second prophecy would come to pass, we will bring you together in one place.”
translations.17:105: And in truth have We sent it down, and in truth has it come down. And We have not sent you but as the giver of good news and as a warner.
translations.17:106: And We have made divisions in the Qur’aan and sent it down in stages, so that you recite it to the people at intervals.
translations.17:107: Say, “Believe in it or not; but they indeed, who are given the knowledge before it, fall down on their faces in humble prostration, when it is recited to them.”
translations.17:108: And they say, “Glorified is our Lord! The prophecy of our Lord was bound to be fulfilled.”
translations.17:109: And they fall on their faces weeping, and it adds to their humility.
translations.17:110: Say, “Pray to Allah or pray to the Gracious One; you may pray to Him by any of His beautiful names. And say your ritual prayer neither too loudly nor in an undertone but choose a suitable tone in between.”
translations.17:111: And say, “To Allah is due all praise, Who has taken no son to Himself. And He has no partner in His absolute sovereignty and has no infirmity to protect against. And do proclaim His greatness!”
translations.18:1: All praise is due to Allah, Who sent down the Book to His devotee and made it flawless.
translations.18:2: That shall remain immutable and straight so that it might warn them of severe punishment from Him and give good news to the believers, who do good deeds, that they shall have a good reward (Paradise).
translations.18:3: They (the believers) shall stay therein for ever.
translations.18:4: And warn those who say, “Allah has a son.”
translations.18:5: They know nothing of it, nor did their fathers know. A grave word it is that comes out of their mouths! They speak nothing but a lie.
translations.18:6: Then maybe you (Prophet) will kill yourself with grief, on their account, if they do not believe in this Pronouncement (Qur’aan). 18:7 We have indeed made whatever is on the earth as an adornment for it, and We put them on trial to see which of them do good deeds.
translations.18:8: And We will indeed turn everything thereon into barren soil.
translations.18:9: Did you perchance consider the Men of the Cave and the story about them as among Our extraordinary signs?
translations.18:10: When the youths sought refuge in the cave, they said, “Our Lord! Grant us mercy from You and facilitate for us the right course in our affair.”
translations.18:11: So We put a seal on their ears for several years in the cave.
translations.18:12: Then We raised them up to see which of the two groups was best able to compute the time they had remained there in their unconscious state.
translations.18:13: We relate to you their story in truth. They were indeed youths who believed in their Lord and We favoured them with more guidance.
translations.18:14: And We strengthened their hearts when they stood up and said, “Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We pray not to any god besides Him; for, then, certainly, we would have uttered an abominable thing.”
translations.18:15: “These, our people, have taken gods besides Him! Why do they not produce any clear evidence in their support? Is there, then, anyone committing a greater wrong than one who concocts a lie against Allah!?”
translations.18:16: “And when you leave them and what they worship besides Allah, go to the cave for refuge. Your Lord will extend His mercy to you and facilitate ease in your affair.”
translations.18:17: And you would see the sun bypass their cave, as it rose in the morning on the right; and as it declined in the afternoon on the left, it would pass them by while they lay in the spacious space in the cave. That is one of the signs of Allah. The one whom Allah guides, is the one rightly guided, and the one whom He misleads, you shall not find for that one any wali to direct him to the right path.
translations.18:18: And you might think them awake, but they were asleep. And We made them turn to the right side and to the left, and their dog squatted on its outstretched forelegs at the entrance. Had you come upon them you would certainly have run away from them, and you would certainly have been overwhelmed with fear of them.
translations.18:19: And so We roused them to make enquiries among themselves. One of them asked, “How long have you been here?” They said, “We have been here for a day or less than a day.” Some said, “Your Lord alone knows how long you have been. Now send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the city and let him find and bring some good food for you. And let him ensure that none comes to know about you.”
translations.18:20: “For indeed if they should find you out, they would stone you to death or force you back into their religion. And, then, you will never get salvation.”
translations.18:21: And thus We made people to stumble upon them (the Men of the Cave) that they (people) might know – when they quarrelled among themselves in their affair – that Allah's promise is true and that there is no doubt about the Hour . And they (the people who came to know the story of the Men of the Cave) said, “Erect a monument over them.” Their Lord is aware of them. Those who had authority over people’s affairs said, “We will certainly raise a place of worship over them.”
translations.18:22: Some of the people would later say, “They (the Men of the Cave) were three, the fourth being their dog.” and some others would say, “Five, the sixth being their dog,” making conjectures about what is unknown. And still others would say, “Seven, and the eighth was their dog.” Say, “My Lord knows their number. None but a few know it. So, argue not about them except for what you do know, and make no enquiries concerning them with any of the people.”
translations.18:23: And never say about anything, “I will indeed do it tomorrow.”
translations.18:24: Without adding, “If Allah pleases.” And remember your Lord when you forget and say, “Maybe my Lord will guide me to better conduct than this.”
translations.18:25: And they remained in their cave for three hundred years and some add nine to the number.
translations.18:26: Say, “Allah knows how long they remained. To Him belong secrets of the heavens and the earth. HE sees them, and He hears! There is no wali for them besides Him, and He does not share His Sovereignty with any one.”
translations.18:27: And recite what has been revealed to you of the Book of your Lord! There is none who can alter His words. And you shall find no provider of shelter besides Him.
translations.18:28: And be yourself patient with those who pray to their Lord, morning and evening, desiring His good pleasure. And let not your eyes stray away from them, desiring glamour of life in this world. And follow not him whose heart We have made neglectful to Our remembrance and who follows his carnal desires and is reckless in his deeds.
translations.18:29: And say, “This is the truth from your Lord.
