So he who wants to, let him believe, and he who wants to, let him suppress the Truth.” We have indeed prepared for those who indulge in wrong-doing a fire, the flames of which shall encompass them. And if they cry for help, they shall be given water like molten brass which will scald their faces. What a miserable drink and what a miserable resting-place!
translations.18:30: We shall indeed not let the reward, of those who believe and do good, go waste.
translations.18:31: These it is for whom are gardens of perpetuity beneath which rivers flow. Reclined comfortably on decorated couches, they will be adorned therein with bracelets of gold, and they will wear green robes of fine silk and heavy brocade. What an excellent reward and how beautiful a resting place!
translations.18:32: And tell them the parable of two men. For one of them We made two gardens of grape vines, and We bordered them both with date-palms. And between the two gardens We made cornfields.
translations.18:33: Each of the gardens yielded its fruits, and nothing went wrong therein. And We caused a spring to gush forth in their midst.
translations.18:34: And he had good yield. So, he said to his companion in a conversation with him, “I have more wealth than you, and I command greater respect among the people.”
translations.18:35: And he entered his garden; and he was unfair to himself. He said, “I do not think that this will ever perish.”
translations.18:36: “And I do not think the Hour will ever occur. And even if I am returned to my Lord I will certainly find there a better place than this.”
translations.18:37: His companion answered, “Do you deny Him Who created you from dust, then from a tiny seed of fertilized ovum and then shaped you into a man?”
translations.18:38: “But as for me, He, Allah, is my Lord, and I do not associate anyone with my Lord.”
translations.18:39: “And why did you not say when you entered your garden that it is as Allah has willed and that there is no power but with Allah? If you see me inferior to you in wealth and children,”
translations.18:40: “Then maybe my Lord will give me something better than your garden and send a thunderbolt from heaven on your garden so that it shall become just a barren land.”
translations.18:41: “Or its waters should sink deep into the ground so that you are unable to find it.”
translations.18:42: And the yield of his gardens was caught in a disastrous wave of destruction. And he started wringing his hands for his investments therein, which had turned upside down. And he said, “Alas! I should not have associated anyone with my Lord.”
translations.18:43: And he had no manpower to help him besides Allah, nor could he help himself.
translations.18:44: There! Allah is the only True Wali . He is best in giving rewards and the best to go to in the end.
translations.18:45: And tell them that the life of this world is like water which We send down from the sky. Spring forth therewith all kinds of vegetation on the earth. Then it (vegetation) becomes dry and brittle and the winds scatter it. And Allah can do all things.
translations.18:46: Wealth and children are but an adornment of the life of this world. And the abiding good deeds are better, for reward and hope, with your Lord.
translations.18:47: And on the Day when We will cause the mountains to move away and you will see the barren earth, We will gather them and leave none of them behind.
translations.18:48: And they shall be lined up before your Lord. “Verily, you have come to Us in the state We created you at first. But you had thought that We had not fixed any appointment for you.”
translations.18:49: And the Book shall be produced. And you will see the sinners scared of its contents. And they will say, “Oh, woe to us! What a book is this! It omits nothing, small or big, but mentions them all.” And whatever they had done, they shall find it there. And your Lord causes no wrong to anyone.
translations.18:50: And when We asked the angels to prostrate to Adam, they all did prostrate, but Iblees did not! He was a jinni, and he swerved from the commandment of his Lord. Would you then take him and his progeny, rather than Me, as awliya , and they are your enemies? What a miserable exchange for the wrongdoers!
translations.18:51: I did not make them witness the creation of the heavens and the earth, nor of the creation of their own selves. And there was no question of my taking those who mislead as helping hands.
translations.18:52: And on the Day when He shall say, “Call those whom you considered to be My associates!” So, they shall call them, but they shall not respond to them. And We shall place a destructive barrier between them.
translations.18:53: And the sinners shall see the Fire, and they shall know that they are going to fall into it. And they shall find no way out there from.
translations.18:54: And We have certainly explained in this Qur’aan, for the benefit of mankind, examples of every kind. And man is, in most things, quarrelsome.
translations.18:55: And nothing prevents people from believing and asking forgiveness of their Lord – when the guidance has already come to them – except that what happened to the ancient peoples should overtake them or that the punishment should confront them.
translations.18:56: And We do not send Messengers but as givers of good news and of warnings. And those who suppress the Truth resort to falsehoods that they may extinguish the truth thereby. And they hold My Verses/signs and warnings in ridicule.
translations.18:57: And who is worse in wickedness than he who is reminded of the Verses/signs of his Lord, but turns away there from and forgets what his two hands have sent before ? We have indeed placed covers over their hearts, and deafness in their ears, lest they should understand it (Lord’s Verses/signs). And if you call them to guidance, they will not follow it ever.
translations.18:58: And your Lord is Forgiving, full of Mercy. Was He to catch them for what they earn, He would certainly have hastened the punishment for them. But there is an appointed time for them from which they shall find no escape.
translations.18:59: And those towns We destroyed when they acted unjustly – We had fixed an appointed time for their destruction!
translations.18:60: And when Moses said to the youth accompanying him, “I will not stop until I reach the confluence of two rivers even if I have to continue this journey for a long time.”
translations.18:61: So when they reached the confluence, they forgot their fish, and it burrowed its way into the sea.
translations.18:62: But when they had gone farther, he said to his young companion, “Take out our breakfast. We have certainly met with fatigue in this journey of ours.”
translations.18:63: The youth said, “You know when we took some rest on the rock, I forgot about the fish – and nothing but the Satan made me forget to tell you about it – when, strangely, it found its way into the river!”
translations.18:64: Moses said, “That was the spot we had sought for!” They then retraced their footsteps back to the spot.
translations.18:65: There they found one of Our devotees. We had favoured him with mercy from Us and We had given him some knowledge from Us.
translations.18:66: Moses said to him, “May I follow you so that you teach me of the right knowledge you have been taught?”
translations.18:67: He said, “You can never have patience with me.”
translations.18:68: “And how can you have patience in matters over which you do not have comprehensive knowledge?”
translations.18:69: He said, “Allah willing, you shall find me patient and I shall not disobey you in any matter.”
translations.18:70: He said, “If you would follow me, question me not about any thing until I myself speak to you about it.”
translations.18:71: And they both went out on a journey. And there arose no problem between them until when they boarded a boat he (Moses’ new companion) made a hole in it. Moses said, “Have you made a hole in it to drown the people thereon? Certainly, you have done a grave thing.”
translations.18:72: Moses’ companion said, “Did I not say that you will not be able to have patience with me?”
translations.18:73: Moses said, “Hold me not responsible for a thing I forgot, and burden me not with difficulty in my affair.”
translations.18:74: And they both went on until, when they met a boy, he (Moses’ companion) killed him. Moses said, “Have you killed an innocent person otherwise than for manslaughter? Certainly, you have done a condemnable thing.”
translations.18:75: Moses’ companion said, “Did I not tell you that you will not be able to have patience with me?”
translations.18:76: Moses said, “If I ask you about anything after this, keep me not in your company. Verily, you shall have then an excuse, provided by me, for you to do so.”
translations.18:77: And they both went on until, when they came to a town, they requested its people to give them some food, but they refused to feed them as guests. They then found in it a wall which was about to collapse, and Moses’ companion set it right. Moses said, “You could certainly have taken some wages for it.”
translations.18:78: Moses’ companion said, “This shall be the parting between me and you. I will now inform you of the true perspective of my actions over which you could not have patience.”
translations.18:79: “As for the boat, it belonged to some poor men who worked on the river. And I wished to make it defective as there was, across them, a king who seized every boat by force.”
translations.18:80: “And as for the boy, his parents were believers, and we feared lest he should trouble them with disobedience and ingratitude.”
translations.18:81: So we desired that their Lord would give them in his place a boy better than him in purity and more compassionate.
translations.18:82: “And as for the wall, it belonged to two orphaned boys in the city, and there was beneath it a treasure belonging to them, and their father was a righteous man. So, your Lord desired that they should attain their maturity and take out their treasure as a mercy from your Lord. And I did not do it of my own accord. That is the true perspective of my actions over which you could not have patience.”
translations.18:83: And they ask you about Zulqarnain . Say, “I will recite an account of him to you.”
translations.18:84: We did indeed establish for him a good position on the earth and bestowed upon him a course of action for everything.
translations.18:85: So, he followed one such course.
translations.18:86: Until, when he reached a place at sunset, he found it (the sun) setting into a spring of muddy water. And he found a people living near it. We said, “O Zulqarnain! (You have the authority) either to punish them or to treat them well.”
translations.18:87: He said, “We will punish only the one who is wicked. He/she shall then be returned to his/her Lord, and He will punish him/her with a severe punishment.”
translations.18:88: And as for the one who believes and does good deeds, he/she shall have a good reward, and We will make Our command easy for him/her.
translations.18:90: Until when he reached a place where, as the sun rose in the East, he found it rise on a community to whom We had given no shelter there from.
translations.18:91: And thus We had put a limit to the knowledge he (Zulqarnain) could have.
translations.18:92: Then he followed yet another course
translations.18:93: And when he reached a place between two mountains, he found beyond these a people who could hardly understand a word.
translations.18:94: They said , “O Zulqarnain! Gog and Magog do indeed make mischief in this land. May we then give you expenses so that you raise a barrier between us and them?”
translations.18:95: Zulqarnain said, “What my Lord has given me is better than what you would give me. So, you assist me with labour, and I will construct an obstruction between you and them.”
translations.18:96: “Bring me iron sheets.” And when he had filled up the gap between the two mountain sides, he said, “Blow!” And when he had turned it red hot, he said, “Bring me molten brass to pour over it.”
translations.18:97: So they were not able to scale it, nor could they breach it.
translations.18:98: He said, “This (construction of the barrier/gate) is a mercy from my Lord. But when the prophecy of my Lord comes to pass, He will level it to the ground, and the prophecy of my Lord has ever to come true.”
translations.18:99: And, one day, We shall let the different communities of mankind get mixed up with one another like waves. And then the trumpet will be blown, and We shall gather them all together.
translations.18:100: And We shall present the Hell, that Day, for display before those who had suppressed the Truth.
translations.18:101: – Those whose eyes had a cover against seeing My Reminder (Qur’aan) and who were unable to listen to it.
translations.18:102: Do they then who suppress the Truth think that they can take My subjects as awliya , besides Me? We have indeed prepared Hell as the destination of those who suppress the Truth.
translations.18:103: Say, “Shall We inform you who the greatest losers are in their deeds?”
translations.18:104: “They are those whose efforts in the life of this world are misdirected and they think that they are doing good work!”
translations.18:105: Those are the people who suppress the Truth in the Verses/signs of their Lord and of the appointment with Him. So, their deeds have become null and void. And so, We will not assign any weightage to their deeds on the Day of Resurrection.
translations.18:106: That Hell is their recompense because they suppressed the Truth and held My Verses/signs and My Messengers in ridicule.
translations.18:107: For those who believe and do good deeds, their bestowal shall indeed be the gardens of paradise.
translations.18:108: They will abide therein; they will never seek any transfer there from.
translations.18:109: Say, “If the sea were ink for the words of my Lord, the sea would surely be exhausted before the words of my Lord are exhausted, though We were to bring the like of it (sea) in aid.”
translations.18:110: Say, “I am only a man like you. It is revealed to me that the Entity you should worship is One and Only. So, whoever hopes to meet his Lord, he should do good deeds, and worship none besides his Lord.”
translations.19:2: This is a narration of the mercy of your Lord on His devotee Zachariah.
translations.19:3: When he prayed to his Lord silently.
translations.19:4: He prayed, “My Lord! My bones have indeed become weak and my head shining white. And, my Lord! I have never been disappointed in my prayer to You.”
translations.19:5: “And indeed I worry about what my relatives will do after me. And my wife is barren. So, bestow upon me a proper heir from You.”
translations.19:6: “Who shall be an heir to me and an heir to the children of Jacob. And make him, my Lord, one in whom You are well pleased!”
translations.19:7: [The divine response was,] “O Zachariah! We do indeed give you the good news of a boy whose name shall be Yahya (John). We had not given this name to anyone before.”
translations.19:8: Zachariah said, “O my Lord! How can I have a son, and my wife is barren, and I have myself grown too old!?”
translations.19:9: [The divine response was,] “So shall it be. Your Lord says, ‘It is not at all difficult for Me, and indeed I created you before, when you were nothing.’”
translations.19:10: Zachariah said, “My Lord! Give me a sign.” The Lord said, “Your sign is that you will not be able to speak to the people for three consecutive nights.”
translations.19:11: So he came out to his people from the chamber, then he communicated to them, through signs, that they should glorify Allah morning and evening.
translations.19:12: “O John! Hold on to the Book firmly.” And We granted him the authority over men while he was yet a child.
translations.19:13: And We gave him the gift of compassion from Us and of purity, and he feared Allah.
translations.19:14: And ever courteous to his parents, he was never insolent, disobedient.
translations.19:15: And peace on him on the day he was born, the day he dies, and the day he is raised to life again.
translations.19:16: And mention Mary in the Book when she withdrew from her family to stay at a place towards the East.
translations.19:17: And she screened herself from them, and We sent to her Our spirit , and it appeared before her as a man well-made.
translations.19:18: She said, “I do indeed seek the Gracious Allah’s protection against you, if you do really fear Allah.”
translations.19:19: He said, “I am a Messenger of your Lord: I am sent to give you a gem of a boy.”
translations.19:20: She said, “How can I have a boy when no man has ever touched me? And I have never been unchaste.”
translations.19:21: He said, “It will be so even in those circumstances – Your Lord says, ‘It is easy for Me.’ – so that We may make him a sign for men, and a mercy from Us. And it is a matter which has already been decreed.”
translations.19:22: So she conceived him and then withdrew with him to a remote place.
translations.19:23: And the throes of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, “Oh, would that I had died before this, and had been a thing long forgotten!”
translations.19:24: Then a voice called out to her from beneath her, “Grieve not, your Lord has surely made a canal flow beneath you.”
translations.19:25: “And shake the trunk of the palm tree towards you. It will drop fresh ripe dates on you.”
translations.19:26: So eat and drink and relax! Then if you should see any man, tell him (by signs), “I have indeed vowed unto the Gracious Allah not to speak to any man today.”
translations.19:27: And she came to her people carrying the child. They said, “O Mary! You have verily done a terrible thing.”
translations.19:28: “O sister of Aaron ! Your father was not a bad man, nor was your mother an unchaste woman.”
translations.19:29: But she pointed to him. They said, “How could we speak to a mere child in cradle?”
translations.19:30: The child said, “I am indeed a devotee of Allah. HE has given me the Book and made me a Prophet.”
translations.19:31: “And He has made me blessed wherever I may be, and He has enjoined on me prayer and charity so long as I live.”
translations.19:32: “And to be couteous to my mother. And He has not made me insolent, impudent.”
translations.19:33: “And peace on me on the day I was born, the day I die and the day I am raised to life again.”
translations.19:34: That (as narrated in Verses 16 to 33 above) then is Jesus, son of Mary – the word of Truth about which they raise disputes.
translations.19:35: It is not befitting the majesty of Allah that He should beget a son. Glorified He is! When He has decreed a matter, He only says to it "Be," and it is.
translations.19:36: And, indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord; hence, worship Him. This is the Straight Path.
translations.19:37: But the tribes disagreed among themselves – and woe to those who disbelieve – about the Court of Judgment to be assembled on a tremendous Day.
translations.19:38: Make them listen to you when you speak to them about the Day they shall appear before Us and make them understand the significance of that Day. But those who are wicked shall continue to remain grossly mistaken about the Day.
translations.19:39: And warn them of the Day of intense regret, when all matters get finally decided. And they are under a delusion and they do not believe.
translations.19:40: We do indeed inherit the earth and all those who are on it, and to Us they all shall be returned.
translations.19:41: And make mention of Abraham in the Book. He was indeed a true Prophet.
translations.19:42: When he said to his father, “O my father! Why do you worship a thing that neither hears nor sees, nor does it give you any benefit whatsoever?”
translations.19:43: “O my father! I do have some knowledge that you do not. So follow me! I will show you the Right Path.”
translations.19:44: “O my father! Worship not the Satan. The Satan is indeed a rebel against Allah, the Gracious.”
translations.19:45: “O my father! I am afraid, a punishment from the Gracious Allah may afflict you and you may befriend the Satan.”
translations.19:46: He (Abraham’s father) said, “Are you averse to my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist I will certainly pelt you with stones. And leave me alone for now.”
translations.19:47: Abraham said, “Peace on you! I will pray to my Lord to forgive you. He is indeed ever benevolent to me.”
translations.19:48: “And I will go away from you and what you pray to besides Allah, and I will pray to my Lord; may be my prayer to my Lord shall not remain unanswered.”
translations.19:49: So when he had gone away from them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob, and We made each a Prophet.
translations.19:50: And We bestowed upon them Our mercy, and We gave them each a highly truthful tongue.
translations.19:51: And mention Moses in the Book. He was indeed pure and clean; and he was a Messenger Prophet.
translations.19:52: And We called to him from the right side of Mount Sinai, and We drew him near for a talk.
translations.19:53: And We made for him, by Our mercy, his brother Aaron a Prophet.
translations.19:54: And mention Ishmael in the Book. He was indeed true to his word, and a Messenger Prophet.
translations.19:55: And he used to enjoin his family to pray and to give in charity, and his Lord was well pleased with him.
translations.19:56: And mention Idris in the Book. He was indeed a true Prophet.
translations.19:57: And We raised him high in status.
translations.19:58: Those were men on whom Allah bestowed favours. Those were from among the Prophets of the seed of Adam, and of those whom We carried in the Ark with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose. Whenever Verses/signs of the Gracious Allah were recited to them, they would fall down prostrating and weeping.
translations.19:59: But then generations that followed them became negligent of prayers and followed sensual desires. And they shall encounter disaster
translations.19:60: Except such as repent and believe and do good deeds. These shall enter the Garden, and no injustice shall be done to them.
translations.19:61: Gardens of perpetuity which the Gracious Allah has promised to those who worship Him even when they do not see Him. His promise shall indeed come to pass.
translations.19:62: They shall not hear therein any vain discourse; the discourse shall be only on peace. And they shall have all their provisions therein morning and evening.
translations.19:63: That is the garden that We cause those of Our subjects to inherit who fear Allah.
translations.19:64: And we (angels) do not descend but by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs whatever is before us and whatever is behind us and whatever is in between. And your Lord does not forget,
translations.19:65: The Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is in between them. So, worship Him and be patient in worshipping Him. Do you know any one equal to Him?
translations.19:66: And says man, “When I am dead and gone, shall I even then certainly be brought out alive?!”
translations.19:67: Does not man remind himself that We had created him before, when he was nothing?
translations.19:68: And, by your Lord, We will most certainly gather them and the satanic people together. Then shall We certainly cause them to be present around Hell on their knees.
translations.19:69: Then We will most certainly pull out from every group one who had transgressed most against Allah, the Gracious.
translations.19:70: And We do certainly know best those who deserve most to be burned therein.
translations.19:71: And there is not one of you but shall come to it. For your Lord this is a decree already issued.
translations.19:72: And We will save (from Hell) those who fear Allah, and We will consign the wrongdoers therein on their knees.
translations.19:73: And when Our clear Verses/signs are recited to them, those who disbelieve say to those who believe, “Which of the two groups of us are better in position and well organised?”
translations.19:74: And how many of the generations have We destroyed before them who were apparently well established!
translations.19:75: Say, “The Gracious Allah will surely give a long lease to all those who have gone astray, until they see what they were warned about: the punishment or the Hour. Then they shall know who is worse in position and weaker in force.”
translations.19:76: And Allah increases guidance of those who go by the Guidance. And lasting good deeds are better for reward with your Lord and yield better return.
translations.19:77: Have you, then, seen him who suppresses the Truth in Our Verses/signs and says, “I shall certainly be given wealth and children?”
translations.19:78: Has he gained knowledge of the unseen, or made a pact with the Gracious Allah?
translations.19:79: By no means! We write down what he says, and We will extend the duration of the punishment for him.
translations.19:80: And We will inherit from him what he talks of (his wealth and his children), and he shall come to Us alone.
translations.19:81: And they have taken gods, besides Allah, as sources of power for them!
translations.19:82: By no means! They shall soon deny their worshipping them, and they shall be adversaries to them.
translations.19:83: Do you not see that We have sent the devils against those who suppress the Truth, inciting them on in their pernicious deeds?
translations.19:84: So be not hasty about them. Their days are numbered.
translations.19:85: The Day on which We shall gather before Allah, the Gracious, groups of pious people.
translations.19:86: And We will drive the criminals in herds towards Hell.
translations.19:87: They shall have no power of intercession, unless one has struck a deal with the Gracious One.
translations.19:88: And they say, “The Gracious One has a son.”
translations.19:89: Certainly you have come up with an atrocious thing.
translations.19:90: The heavens almost were torn asunder thereat, the earth was disturbed, and the mountains collapsed devastatingly
translations.19:91: At that they ascribed a son to the Gracious One!
translations.19:92: And it is inconceivable that the Gracious One could have a son. 19:93 None in the heavens and the earth but will come to the Gracious One obediently as a slave.
translations.19:94: Certainly He has counted them and He has given everyone a number.
translations.19:95: And all of them will come to Him, individually, on the Day of Resurrection.
translations.19:96: The Gracious One (Allah) will certainly create affection for those who believe and do good deeds.
translations.19:97: And then We have made it (the Qur’aan) easy in your tongue only so that you may give good news thereby to those who fear Allah and warn thereby a hostile people.
translations.19:98: And how many a generation before them have We destroyed! Do you have any perception of any of them or hear any sound from them?
translations.20:2: We have not sent down the Qur’aan upon you to cause you distress.
translations.20:3: It is but a Memorandum for one who fears Allah.
translations.20:4: It is a bestowal from the One Who created the earth and the lofty heavens.
translations.20:5: From the Gracious One, firmly established on the throne of absolute authority.
translations.20:6: Everything – that which is in the heavens, on the earth, in between the two, and underneath the ground – is all His.
translations.20:7: And if you say anything aloud, mind you, He knows the secret, and the hidden!
translations.20:8: Allah – there is no god but He; His are the very best attributes.
translations.20:9: And has Moses’ story come down to you?
translations.20:10: When he saw some fire, he said to his wife escorting him, “Wait! I do indeed see a fire. Maybe, I can bring to you there from a live burning coal or come to know about the right way from someone at the fire.”
translations.20:11: And when he came to it (the fire), he was called out, “O Moses!”
translations.20:12: “Verily, I am your Lord; so, take off your shoes! You are indeed in the sacred valley of Tuwa.”
translations.20:13: “And I have chosen you, so then listen to what is revealed.”
translations.20:14: “Indeed, I, Myself, am Allah! There is no god but I. So worship Me and establish regular prayer to remember Me.”
translations.20:15: “The Hour (Last Day) is indeed coming. I will unravel it anytime so that every soul is recompensed for what it strived.”
translations.20:16: “So let him not, who does not believe in it (the Hour) and follows his low desires, turn you away from it so that you should perish.”
translations.20:17: “And what is that in your right hand, Moses!”
translations.20:18: Moses said, “It is my staff. I lean upon it and I beat the leaves with it to make them fall upon my sheep, and I have other uses for it.”
translations.20:19: Allah said, “Cast it down, O Moses!”
translations.20:20: And as Moses cast his staff down, it instantly became a serpent moving!
translations.20:21: Allah said, “Hold it and be not afraid! We will restore it to its original state.”
translations.20:22: “And, another sign: put your hand under your arm, it shall come out perfectly white!”
translations.20:23: “That We may show you some of Our great signs.”
translations.20:24: “Go to Pharaoh! He has indeed exceeded all bounds.”
translations.20:25: He said, “O my Lord! Broaden my mind for me.”
translations.20:26: “And make my task easy for me.” ”
translations.20:27: “And correct the stammer in my speech. 20: 28. “Make them understand what I say.”
translations.20:29: “And appoint, from my family, an assistant for me.”
translations.20:32: “And make him my associate in my work.”
translations.20:33: “So that we should glorify You much.”
translations.20:36: Allah said, “Your petition stands granted, O Moses!”
translations.20:37: "And We did confer a favour on thee another time.”
translations.20:38: “When We revealed to your mother that which was revealed.”
translations.20:39: “That she should put him into a chest, and then put the chest onto the river. Then the river should throw him on the shore. There, one, who is an enemy to Me and enemy to him, should pick him up. And I made you lovable so that you might be brought up under My watchful eye.”
translations.20:40: “When your sister went and said, ‘May I lead you to one who will feed him?’ Thus We returned you to your mother so that she should be comforted and not grieved. And you killed a man, and then We delivered you from that worry. And We tried you variously. Then you stayed for years among the people of Madyan. Then you came here as ordained, O Moses!”
translations.20:41: “And I have made you for Myself.”
translations.20:42: “Now go, you and your brother, with My signs and be not remiss in remembering Me!”
translations.20:43: “Go both of you to Pharaoh! He has indeed committed transgression.”
translations.20:44: “And speak to him gently. Maybe he takes heed or refrains from further evil.”
translations.20:45: Both said, “O our Lord! We do indeed fear that he may take no heed of us or that he may commit some transgression.”
translations.20:46: Allah said, “Fear not! I, Myself, am indeed with you both; I hear, and I see.”
translations.20:47: “So go you both to him and say, ‘We two are, indeed, Messengers of your Lord. Send the Children of Israel with us and torment them not! Verily, we have brought to you a sign from your Lord. And peace on him who follows the guidance!’
translations.20:48: ‘It has indeed been revealed to us that punishment will come upon him who rejects and turns away.’”
translations.20:49: Pharaoh asked, “And who is the Lord of you two, O Moses?”
translations.20:50: Moses said, “Our Lord is He Who gave everything its creation, and then guided it.”
translations.20:51: Pharaoh asked, “Then what about the earlier generations?”
translations.20:52: Moses said, “The knowledge thereof is in a record with my Lord. My Lord errs not, nor does He forget.”
translations.20:53: The Lord Who made the earth for you a flattened expanse and laid for you therein paths. And He sent down water from the sky. Then thereby We have brought out diverse herbs/plants in pairs.
translations.20:54: Eat and tend your cattle. There are indeed signs in that for those who are prudent.
translations.20:55: From it (earth) We created you and into it We shall send you back and from it will We raise you a second time.
translations.20:56: And verily We showed him Our signs, all of them, but he rejected and turned arrogant.
translations.20:57: Pharaoh said, “Have you come to drive us out of our land by your magic, O Moses?”
translations.20:58: “We shall certainly then bring to you similar magic. Let us then, between us and you, have an appointment at a mutually convenient place, which we should neither of us fail to keep.”
translations.20:59: Moses said, “Let your appointment be on the day of the Festival and let the people be gathered together in the morning.”
translations.20:60: So Pharaoh withdrew, devised his plan of action, and then came.
translations.20:61: Moses said to them , “Woe to you! Do not fabricate a lie against Allah, lest He should destroy you by a punishment. And he, who fabricates, does indeed fail.”
translations.20:62: And the magicians debated among themselves their response to Moses, but kept the discourse secret.
translations.20:63: They said (among themselves), “Indeed, these two are just magicians. They wish to turn you out of your land by their magic and to deprive you of your best traditions.”
translations.20:64: “So do combine your plan, and then come in line. And he that is superior will certainly win this day.”
translations.20:65: They said (aloud), “O Moses! Will you do your act, or shall we be the first to do it?”
translations.20:66: He said, “No! You do it.” And lo! Their ropes and their sticks appeared to him (Moses), by their magic, as if they were moving.
translations.20:67: So Moses felt a fear rising within him.
translations.20:68: WE said, “Fear not, you shall indeed have the upper hand.”
translations.20:69: “And cast down that which is in your right hand. It shall devour what they have contrived. They have contrived only what a magician could plan. And the magician shall not succeed wherever he may have come from.”
translations.20:70: And the magicians were obliged to fall prostrate! They said, “We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.”
translations.20:71: Pharaoh said, “You believed in him before I gave you leave. He ought to have been your superior who taught you magic. I will then certainly cut off your hands and your feet from opposite sides, and I will certainly crucify you on the trunks of the palm trees. And certainly you will come to know which of us is the more severe and the more persistent in punishing.”
translations.20:72: The magicians said, “There is no question of our preferring you over Him Who made us, after what has come to us of clear signs. So, decide whatever you want to. You can only decide about life in this world.”
translations.20:73: “We do indeed believe in our Lord. May He forgive us our sins and that magic which you forced upon us. And Allah is better and everlasting.”
translations.20:74: Whoever comes to his Lord as a sinner, for him indeed is Hell. He shall not die therein, nor shall he live.
translations.20:75: And whoever comes to Him as a believer with righteous deeds to his credit, he shall be among those who shall enjoy high ranks.
translations.20:76: Gardens of perpetuity, beneath which rivers flow, to abide therein. And that is the reward of one who purifies oneself.
translations.20:77: And We did reveal to Moses that he should “travel by night with My devotees, then make for them a dry path in the sea, without any fear of being overtaken or of any other sort.”
translations.20:78: And Pharaoh followed them with his armies. And so there came upon them from the sea that which came upon them.
translations.20:79: And Pharaoh led his people astray and guided them not.
translations.20:80: O Children of Israel! We did verily deliver you from your enemy, and We made a covenant with you on the right side of the mountain, and We sent to you the manna and the quails.
translations.20:81: Make use of the good things We have provided you with and be not inordinate therein lest My wrath should become due upon you. And he, upon whom My wrath becomes due, certainly perishes.
translations.20:82: And I am indeed most forgiving to him who repents and believes and does good deeds, and then follows the right direction.
translations.20:83: “And what made you come here earlier than your people, O Moses?”
translations.20:84: Moses said, “They are close behind me. And I hastened to You, my Lord, that You might be pleased.”
translations.20:85: Allah said, “But then We put your people on trial after you had left them behind, and the Samiri has led them astray.”
translations.20:86: Then Moses returned to his people, angry and sorrowful. He Said, “O my people! Did not your Lord give you a good promise? Did then the time for fulfillment of the promise seem too long to you? Or did you wish to incur wrath from your Lord, so you broke your promise to me?
translations.20:87: They said, “We did not break our promise to you of our own accord, but we were made to bear the burden of the ornaments of the people. So, we got rid of that burden just as the Samiri did.”
translations.20:88: Then he (Samiri) brought out for them a body of a calf , from which emanated a low sound. Then he told them, “This is your god and the god of Moses. But he (Moses) has forgotten it.”
translations.20:89: Could they not see that it did not respond to them at all, and that it did have no power to do any harm or to bring any benefit to them?
translations.20:90: And certainly had Aaron told them earlier, “O my people! You are only being tried therewith. And, indeed, your Lord is the Gracious One. Follow me then and obey my order!”
translations.20:91: They had said, “We will not abandon worshipping it until Moses returns to us.”
translations.20:92: Moses said, “O Aaron! What prevented you, when you saw them going astray,
translations.20:93: From following me? Did you then go against my instruction?”
translations.20:94: Aaron said, “O son of my mother! Seize me not by my beard and by my head. I was indeed afraid you might say, ‘You have caused a rift among the children of Israel and not waited for my word.’”
translations.20:95: Moses then asked, “What have you to say on this, O Samiri?”
translations.20:96: He said, “I saw what they did not see. And I took a handful of traces from the messenger and flung it. And that is what my own self prompted me to do.”
translations.20:97: Moses said, “Begone! Indeed, it is ordained for you in this life to say, ‘Touch me not!’ And indeed there is a prediction for you that shall not fail. And look at your god that you hovered around! We will certainly burn it. Then we will certainly reduce it to dust and scatter it in the sea.”
translations.20:98: None but Allah is worthy of your worship! He knows everything.
translations.20:99: Thus do We narrate to you some stories from the past. And We have verily given you the account (in the Qur’aan) from Ourselves.
translations.20:100: One who turns oneself away there from shall indeed bear a burden on the Day of Resurrection.
translations.20:101: Burdened they shall remain. And too bad for them the burden, On the Day of Resurrection!
translations.20:102: The Day, the trumpet is blown – the Day, We gather the sinful, blue with fear.
translations.20:103: Whispering among them, “You stayed but ten days.”
translations.20:104: We know best what they say; and the one among them, with the exemplary method of giving estimates, would say, “You stayed but a day.”
translations.20:105: And they ask you about the mountains. Say, “My Lord will pull them down to their roots.”
translations.20:106: And reduce it to a smooth, levelled plain.
translations.20:107: You shall not see therein any ups and downs.
translations.20:108: That Day they shall follow the caller, and they shall have no alternative but to follow him. And the voices shall be low in deference to the Gracious One and you shall not hear but a soft murmur.
translations.20:109: That day no intercession shall benefit anyone except from him whom the Gracious One permits and whose word of intercession He approves.
translations.20:110: HE knows what they are aware of, and what they are not aware of, and what is beyond the sphere of their knowledge.
translations.20:111: And the faces shall be bent down before the Living, Everlasting One. And he who bears the burden of wrongdoing is certainly doomed.
translations.20:112: And he who does some good work and is also a believer, he shall have no fear of injustice or of usurpation.
translations.20:113: And so have We sent it down as a Qur’aan in Arabic , and have detailed therein some of the things that are going to happen in the future so that they may fear Allah and take heed or that it may serve them as a Reminder of things that have gone by.
translations.20:114: And exalted is Allah, the true Sovereign! And make no haste with reciting the Qur’aan before its revelation to you is completed. And say, “O my Lord! Increase my knowledge.”
translations.20:116: And when We asked the angels to prostrate before Adam, prostrated they all except for Iblees. He (Iblees) refused!
translations.20:117: And We said, “O Adam! This is an enemy to you and to your wife; so, let him not drive you both out from the garden and thus make you miserable.”
translations.20:118: “It is indeed ordained for you that you shall not be hungry therein nor be naked.”
translations.20:119: “And that you shall not be thirsty therein nor shall you feel hot.”
translations.20:120: Then the Satan seduced him, saying, “O Adam! Shall I show you the tree of immortality and a kingdom that shall not decay?”
translations.20:121: And they both succumbed to the satanic seduction, and then their sexuality got opened to them, and they both began to cover themselves with leaves of the garden. And Adam disobeyed his Lord and thus deviated from the right path.
translations.20:122: Then his Lord chose him , so He accepted his repentance and guided him.
translations.20:123: Allah said, “Down you both (Adam & Satan) go, together, in reciprocal enmity. And whenever Guidance comes to you from Me, then all those who follow My Guidance shall not go astray or be miserable.”
translations.20:124: “And he who turns away from My Reminder shall lead a hard life, and We will raise him on the Day of Resurrection, blind.”
translations.20:125: He will say, “My Lord! Why have You raised me blind? And I was certainly one able to see!”
translations.20:126: Allah will say, “Just as Our Verses/signs came to you, but you neglected them, so shall you be neglected this day.”
translations.20:127: And thus, do We recompense him who is negligent and believes not in the Verses/signs of his Lord. And certainly, the punishment in the Hereafter is severe and abiding.
translations.20:128: Do they not take a lesson from the fact that We had destroyed many a generation before theirs, in whose dwelling-places they now move about? Indeed, there are signs in that for those who are prudent.
translations.20:129: And had it not been that the Word had already gone forth from your Lord and an appointed term fixed, the same fate (of destruction in this world) that the earlier generations met with, would certainly be theirs (the generation addressed to in this Verse) too.
translations.20:130: Exercise patience then over what they say. And glorify your Lord with His praise before the rising of the sun and before its going down. And glorify Him, during the night and during parts of the day, so that you get peace of mind.
translations.20:131: And covet not that – which We have provided some couples among them with – of the glitter of this worldly life, whereby We try them. And what the Lord provides you with is better and lasting.
translations.20:132: And enjoin prayer on your followers and be regular and steadfast thereon. We do not ask you for any provision; We give it to you. And in the end, it is those who fear Allah, and abide by His instructions, that get all the good things to enjoy.
translations.20:133: And they say, “Why does he not bring to us a sign from his Lord?” Has not there come to them a clear exposition of what the earlier scriptures contain?
translations.20:134: And had We destroyed them with punishment before this, they would certainly have said, “O our Lord! Why did You not send us a Messenger? For, we should then have followed Your Verses/signs before we met disgrace and shame.”
translations.20:135: Say, “Everyone is waiting, so you too wait! You will soon come to know who is following the right path and who is guided.”
translations.21:1: For the human beings, their time of reckoning is near, and yet they pass their time in utter recklessness.
translations.21:2: No aspect of divinity comes to them from their Lord in the form of a fresh anecdote, but they listen to it in jest
translations.21:3: Just to serve as a distraction for their minds. And the wicked people secretly whisper, “Is he not just a man like you? Will you then knowingly yield to the magic spell?”
translations.21:4: The Prophet said, “My Lord knows every word spoken anywhere in the heavens and the earth. And He is the One to hear and the One to know everything.”
translations.21:5: But they variously said, “Just a confusing mixture of dreams!” “But he has forged it.” “But he is a poet.” “Let him then bring to us a sign just as the earlier prophets were sent with.”
translations.21:6: Any town/village that We had destroyed before them, too, had not believed! Will they, taking this fact into account at least, believe?
translations.21:7: And We sent (as Prophets/Messengers for mankind), before you, none but men inspired with Our Revelation. Ask the people of the Reminder (divinely revealed Book) if you do not know.
translations.21:8: And We had not made them bodies that did not eat food, and they were not immortal.
translations.21:9: And We made Our promise good to them; We saved them and those whom We pleased, and We destroyed the transgressors.
translations.21:10: Verily We have revealed to you a Book in which are things for you to ponder over, remember and abide by. Do you then not use your intelligence to understand it?
translations.21:11: And many a town/village, whose inhabitants were cruel, did We shatter, and We raised up, thereafter, another people!
translations.21:12: No sooner did they perceive Our punishment coming than they began to flee there from.
translations.21:13: Flee not and come back to the affluence you were accustomed to live in and to your homes for being questioned!
translations.21:14: They said, “O woe to us! We were wicked.”
translations.21:15: And that cry of theirs continued till We made them like smouldering harvested field.
translations.21:16: And We did not create the heavens and the earth, and what is between them, for fun.
translations.21:17: Had We wished to have some amusement, We would have derived it from within Ourselves, if at all.
translations.21:18: Nay! We cast the truth against the falsehood to demolish it (falsehood). And lo! Falsehood perishes. And woe to you for the (wrong) way you perceive things.
translations.21:19: And whoever is in the heavens and the earth is His. And those who are with Him are never too proud to worship Him, nor do they get weary.
translations.21:20: They glorify Allah, night and day, without a break.
translations.21:21: Or have they taken gods whom they themselves have fashioned/designed out of the earth!?
translations.21:22: Had there been in them any gods other than Allah, the two (the heavens and the earth) would certainly have been in a state of disorder. Glorified then is Allah, the Lord of the Throne , above all that they attribute to Him.
translations.21:23: He cannot be questioned on what He does, and they (human beings) shall be questioned on what they do!
translations.21:24: Or, have they taken gods other than Him? Say, “Produce your evidence! This is the precept (that there is no god besides Allah) for those with me and the precept for those before me.” But most of them do not know the truth, and so they are intransigent.
translations.21:25: And We did not send before you any Messenger, but We revealed to him that there is no god but I, and so worship Me.
translations.21:26: And they say, “The Gracious One has begotten (angels)!” Glorified is He. Nay! They are His honoured worshippers.
translations.21:27: They do not speak ahead of Him and they act at His Command.
translations.21:28: HE knows what they know of and what they do not know of. And they intercede not except for him whom He wills and they are on their guard in awe of Him.
translations.21:29: And whoever of them says that he is indeed a god besides Him, We award Hell to such a one. We do thus award the wicked people.
translations.21:30: Do those who suppress the Truth not see that the heavens and the earth were both one joint entity, and then We tore them apart. And We have made of water everything living, will they not then believe?
translations.21:31: And We have placed mountains in the earth so that positions thereon do not move. And We have made therein passes to enable people to move about and find their way.
translations.21:32: And We have made the sky a protective roof and yet they are oblivious to its signs!
translations.21:33: And He it is Who created the night and the day, the sun and the moon. All in orbit float.
translations.21:34: And We did not ordain immortality for any man before you. And so, if you die, will they remain alive?
translations.21:35: Everyone must taste death. And We test you with trial periods of adversity and prosperity. And to Us you shall be brought back.
translations.21:36: And when those who suppress the Truth see you, they do not take you but for one to be scoffed at, “Is this the one who talks of your gods?” And they suppress mention of the Gracious One.
translations.21:37: Man is created hasty in nature. Soon shall I show you My signs. So be not hasty!
translations.21:38: And they ask, “When will this prediction come to pass if what you say is true?”
translations.21:39: Had those who suppress the Truth but known that a time would come when they shall not be able to ward off the fire from their faces or from their backs! And then they shall get no help.
translations.21:40: But it (the time mentioned in Verse 39 above) shall come on them suddenly and catch them unawares. And they shall not be able to avert it. And they shall not be given any respite.
translations.21:41: And certainly Messengers before you were scoffed at. But then the thing they mocked about surrounded those of them who scoffed.
translations.21:42: Ask them, “Who can guard you, by night and by day, from the Gracious One?” And, yet, they turn away at the mention of their Lord.
translations.21:43: Or, have they gods who can defend them against Us? They shall not be able to help themselves. Nor shall they have any benefit of association with Us.
translations.21:44: But, for a prolonged period in their lives, We did provide for them and their fathers with good things of life. Have they therefore become so complacent as not to see that We are closing in on them from all sides? And does their complacency make them think that they shall still prevail?
translations.21:45: Tell them, “I warn you only because I have been divinely instructed to do so.” And the deaf do not hear the call howsoever much they are warned.
translations.21:46: And if punishment of your Lord were to touch them even slightly, they would certainly say, “O woe to us! We did indeed commit wrong.”
translations.21:47: And We will set up just scales for the Day of Resurrection, so no wrong shall be done to anyone. And if there be anything as little in weight as that of a mustard seed, We will present it nevertheless. And We are sufficiently proficient in keeping accounts.
translations.21:48: And certainly We did give Moses and Aaron the Criterion, a Light, and a Book often to refer to for guidance to those who would fear Allah and be pious.
translations.21:49: Those that feared their Lord without seeing Him and were anxiously concerned about the Hour (marking the end of this world and heralding the Day of Judgment in the Hereafter).
translations.21:50: And would you then deny that this (Qur’aan) is the blessed Book, often to refer to for guidance, We have now revealed?
translations.21:51: And certainly We did give Abraham, before (Moses and Aaron), his firmly right conduct. And We were aware of this
translations.21:52: When he asked his father and his people, “What statues are these that you are so devoted to?”
translations.21:53: They said, “We found our fathers worshipping them.”
translations.21:54: Abraham said, “Certainly you had – you and your fathers – gone far astray.”
translations.21:55: They said, “Are you telling us the truth, or just making fun of us?”
translations.21:56: He said, “But your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. It is He Who created them out of nothing. And I am of those who bear witness to this.”
translations.21:57: “And, by Allah! I will certainly do something to your idols after you go away, turning your backs.”
translations.21:58: So he broke those (idols) into pieces, except for the biggest of them, that they (polytheists) may turn to it (to inform them as to who did the destruction of the other idols).
translations.21:59: They asked, “Who has done this to our gods? The one who did this is certainly one of those most wicked!”
translations.21:60: Some said, “We had heard a youth called Abraham speaking about them.”
translations.21:61: The others said, “Then bring him before the eyes of the people. Let all witness.”
translations.21:62: The polytheists said, “Have you done this to our gods, O Abraham!?”
translations.21:63: Abraham said, “Nay, it is this one – the chief of them – that has done it. So, ask them, if they can speak coherently!”
translations.21:64: Then, turning to themselves, they said, “You yourselves indeed are in the wrong.”
translations.21:65: And, their heads hanging down, they said to Abraham, “You do know they do not speak.”
translations.21:66: Abraham said, “Do you then worship, besides Allah, that which brings you no benefit at all, and no harm either?”
translations.21:67: “Fie on you and on that which you worship besides Allah! Why do you not use your intellect?”
translations.21:68: They said, “Put him in the fire and help your gods, if you are going to do anything.”
translations.21:69: WE ordered, “O fire! Be you a source of cool comfort and peace on Abraham.” 21:70 And they had designed a plot against him, but We made them themselves the doomed ones.
translations.21:71: And We saved him and Lot and led them to the land in which We had placed Our blessings for all.
translations.21:72: And We gave him Isaac – and, in addition, Jacob – and We made them righteous.
translations.21:73: And We made them leaders who guided people by Our command. And We revealed to them how good deeds are done, how prayers are performed, and how charities are given. And Us certainly they obeyed.
translations.21:74: And Lot – We gave him wisdom and knowledge. And We delivered him from the town that did bad things. They were indeed a wicked, transgressing people (indulging in the sin of homosexuality).
translations.21:75: And We took him (Lot) under Our mercy. He was indeed one of the righteous men.
translations.21:76: And Noah – when he called out in an earlier period, We answered him, and delivered him and his family from the acute suffering (of drowning).
translations.21:77: And We helped him against the people who rejected Our Verses/signs. They were indeed a wicked people, and so We drowned them all.
translations.21:78: And David and Solomon – when they gave their judgments in the matter of the field wherein the sheep of some people pastured by night. And We bore witness to their judgments.
translations.21:79: And We made Solomon to understand the problem better; but We gave wisdom and knowledge to each one of them. And We made the mountains, and the birds to sing Our praise with David (in the Psalms ). And We did all that.
translations.21:80: And We taught him (David) how to make for you armours that fortify you in your battles. Are you then grateful?
translations.21:81: And to Solomon We made subservient the stormy wind, which blew at his command to the land We had blessed. And We did know all things.
translations.21:82: And of the devils there were those who dived for him and did other work besides. And We were guardians over them.
translations.21:83: And Job – as he cried to his Lord, “A calamity indeed it is that has afflicted me! And You are the most Merciful.”
translations.21:84: And We responded to him and removed whatever affliction he had. And We gave him his family, and as many more with them, out of mercy from Us and for Allah’s worshippers to remember. 21:85 And Ishmael and Idris and Zulkifl – they were all of those who were patient.
translations.21:86: And We admitted them to Our Mercy. They were indeed among the righteous people.
translations.21:87: And the man of the fish – when he went away in anger , thinking We would not punish him! Then, among the darknesses (of the fish belly), he cried, “There is no god but You, glorified You are! I have indeed been of those who do wrong.”
translations.21:88: Then We responded to him and delivered him from the grief. And thus, do We deliver those who believe.
translations.21:89: And Zachariah – when he cried to his Lord, “O my Lord! Leave me not alone (without an heir). And You are the best of inheritors.”
translations.21:90: And We responded to him and gave him John (Yahya) and made his wife fit to bear the child. They did indeed try to surpass one another in deeds of goodness and they prayed to Us in hope and fear. And they were humble before Us.
translations.21:91: And the lady (Mary) – who guarded her chastity, and We breathed into her of Our spirit and We made her and her son a sign for the worlds.
translations.21:92: Indeed this community of yours is one community, and I am your Lord! So, worship Me.
translations.21:94: Then one who performs some good deeds and is a believer too – there shall be no concealment of his/her efforts! And surely indeed We will record it (the good deeds).
translations.21:95: And an injunction against any village/town that We have destroyed: they shall not return!
translations.21:96: Until the time Gog and Magog are let loose and they descend from every prominent position.
translations.21:97: And the true promise (of Resurrection) shall draw nigh. Then lo! The eyes of those who suppressed the Truth shall open wide in fear. They shall say, “O woe to us! We certainly were heedless of this; nay, we were wicked.”
translations.21:98: You and what you worship besides Allah are indeed the fuel of Hell-fire; to it you shall come.
translations.21:99: Had these been gods, they would not have come to it (Hell) and all shall be there eternally.
translations.21:100: For them therein wailing and they therein shall not hear.
translations.21:101: Indeed those for whom the good has already gone forth from Us shall be kept far off from it (Hell).
translations.21:102: They will not hear its (Hell’s) faintest sound, and they shall for ever be in what they desire.
translations.21:103: The great fearful event shall not grieve them, and the angels shall meet and tell them, “This is your promised Day!” 21:104 The Day We roll up heaven like the scroll of records is rolled up! As did We originate the first creation, so shall We revive it! A promise binding on Us. We will indeed fulfill it.
translations.21:105: And certainly We did write in the Zabur , after the recounting of events, that My righteous worshippers shall inherit the earth.
translations.21:106: Indeed in this is a message for people who worship.
translations.21:107: And We have not sent you but as a Mercy to the worlds.
translations.21:108: Say, “It is revealed to me that the Entity Whom you all ought to worship is but One and Only [Allah]! Will you then submit?”
translations.21:109: But if they turn away, say, “I have given you a warning in fairness and I know not what you are warned of is near or far.”
translations.21:110: “HE does indeed know what is spoken openly and He knows what you hide.”
translations.21:111: “And I know not but that this (your life in this world) may be a trial for you and a provision to enjoy for a time.”
translations.21:112: He (the Prophet) said, “O my Lord! Decide justly (between me and my recalcitrant people). And our Lord is the Gracious One – the One Whose help is sought against what you (the recalcitrant people) say.”
translations.22:1: O people! Fear your Lord. Indeed, the cataclysm of the Hour is a tremendous thing.
translations.22:2: That day, were you to witness it, every woman giving suck shall abandon what she suckled, and every pregnant woman shall abort what she bore. And you would see humans appear intoxicated, and they shall not be intoxicated. But the cataclysmic Act of Allah will be so severe (as to make them appear intoxicated).
translations.22:3: And among mankind, one who disputes about Allah without knowledge and follows every rebellious satan;
translations.22:4: Against such a one, it is ordained that he/she shall lead astray – and guide towards the punishment of the Fire – whoever takes him/her for a very close friend ( wali ).
translations.22:5: O mankind! If you are in doubt about the Resurrection, then – to make things clear to you – We did indeed create you from dust, then from a little seed of fertilized ovum, then from a leech-like clinging clot of curdled blood, then from a chewed-like lump of flesh (foetus) with formed and unformed parts. And We facilitate what We will in the wombs till an appointed time, and then We bring you out as babies. Then We facilitate conditions for you to attain to your maturity. And of you is he who is made to die, and of you is he who is reduced to the worst part of life (very old age), so that he does not remember anything of the knowledge he had acquired earlier. And you see the earth barren, but when We send down the water on it, it stirs to life and flourishes and produces every kind of a beautiful spouse of herbage.
translations.22:6: That (Resurrection) is bound to happen because Allah is the Truth and because He gives life to the dead and because He has power over all things.
translations.22:7: And because the Hour is coming and there is no doubt about it; and because Allah shall raise up those who are in the graves.
translations.22:8: And among mankind there is such a one as disputes about Allah without knowledge, without guidance and without an enlightening book.
translations.22:9: Such a one turns his face away in pride from divine guidance that he may lead others astray from Allah’s Path. For him is disgrace in this world. And on the Day of Resurrection We will make him taste punishment in the Hell.
translations.22:10: “That punishment is for what your two hands have sent before.” And remember that Allah is not in the least unjust to those whom He has created for His worship.
translations.22:11: And among mankind is he who worships Allah sitting on the fence. Then if anything good happens to him he is satisfied with that. But if he is put to trial with some adversity he turns his face away in disgust. He is doomed here and he is doomed in the Hereafter. That is the doom manifest!
translations.22:12: He prays to someone – other than Allah – who can neither harm nor benefit him. That is going far, far astray!
translations.22:13: He prays to him whose harm is closer than his benefit. Evil certainly is such guardian and evil certainly is such associate.
translations.22:14: Allah will indeed cause those who believe and do good deeds to enter gardens beneath which rivers flow. Allah does indeed do what He wills.
translations.22:15: If anyone thinks that Allah does not help him in this life and the Hereafter, let him reach out to the heavens by means available to him, then (study its functions meticulously by) cutting himself off (his earthly connection), and then see if his obstinacy gets rid of its severity.
translations.22:16: And thus have We revealed it (the Qur’aan) with Verses made clear. And Allah it is Who guides whom He wills.
translations.22:17: Those who believe (in the Qur’aan) and those who are Jews and the Saabieen and the Christians and the Magians (Zoroastrians or Parsis or fire-worshippers) and the polytheists – Allah will indeed decide between them on the day of Resurrection. Allah indeed is a witness to all things.
translations.22:18: Do you not see that it is Allah to Whom prostrates whatever is in the heavens and whatever in the earth – the sun, the moon, the stars, the mountains, the trees, the living creatures and many among mankind. And among mankind there are many upon whom punishment has become due. And whomsoever Allah abases, there is none who can make him honourable. Allah does indeed do what He wills.
translations.22:19: These are two adversaries who dispute about their Lord. And garments of fire shall be cut out for those who suppress the Truth, with boiling water being poured over their heads.
translations.22:20: What there is in their bellies, and skins, shall be melted therewith.
translations.22:21: And for them there shall be cages of iron.
translations.22:22: Whenever they will, in their anguish, desire to go out from it, they shall be pushed back into it and told, “Taste punishment by the Fire.”
translations.22:23: Allah will indeed admit those who believe and do good deeds into gardens beneath which rivers flow. They shall be adorned therein with bracelets of gold and pearls, and their garments therein shall be of silk.
translations.22:24: And they will be guided to say good things while speaking and they will be guided onto the Path of the Praised One.
translations.22:25: As regards those indeed who suppress the Truth and hinder people away from Allah's Path and from the Sacred Place of Worship – which We have made open equally for all mankind, for the local resident as well as for the visitor – We shall make him taste a painful punishment who intends making unjust and wrongful deviations therein .
translations.22:26: And when We assigned to Abraham the location of the House (at Makkah), Allah told him, “Worship not anyone but Me, and sanctify My House for those who take the rounds thereof and stand in prayer and bow and prostrate.”
translations.22:27: And proclaim among mankind the duty of performing the Pilgrimage. Let them come to you on foot and on every means of conveyance. They will come from every distant broad way.
translations.22:28: That they may witness benefits for them and take Allah’s name, during days appointed, over what He has provided for them of the quadruped cattle. Then they eat thereof and feed the distressed, the needy.
translations.22:29: Then let them attend to personal hygiene like cutting superfluous hair and nails and let them fulfil their vows (like praying for their near and dear ones back home) and let them take rounds of the Ancient House.
translations.22:30: And so it is better for anyone, in the eyes of his Lord, that he honours Allah’s injunctions. And the cattle – except for what are mentioned to you – are made lawful for you. Avoid then the abomination of the idols and utter no falsehood.
translations.22:31: Stand firm and upright for Allah, and worship none but Him. And he who worships anyone/anything besides Allah, it is as though he had fallen from the skies, and the bird has snatched him away or the wind carried him off to a distant place.
translations.22:32: And so when anyone honours the sacred rites/signs sanctioned by Allah, then it is of the acts that make hearts pious.
translations.22:33: You have benefits in them (cattle brought for sacrifice during the Hajj) for a time, and then their destination is the Ancient House.
translations.22:34: And for every community We appointed a sacrificial rite that they may remember and mention Allah’s name over what He has given them of the quadruped cattle. The Entity worthy of your worship then is One and Only. So, to Him you all should submit. And give good tidings to the humble.
translations.22:35: Those whose hearts tremble when Allah is mentioned, and those who are patient over what afflicts them, and those who keep up the prayer as prescribed, and spend appropriately (as prescribed in the Qur’aan) out of what We have given them.
translations.22:36: And the camel! We have made it one of those things (emblems) through which one could come to know Allah. And there is much good in it for you. Mention Allah’s name over it as it is lined up (for its sacrifice during the Hajj), and then when it falls on its sides, eat there from and feed the contented as well as the poor. We have thus made it subservient to you, in order that you be grateful.
translations.22:37: Its (the sacrificed animal’s) flesh reaches not Allah, nor does its blood. But it is your piety that reaches Him. He has thus made it subservient to you, in order that you may glorify Allah for what He has gifted to you. And give glad tidings to those who do handsome good deeds .
translations.22:38: Allah does indeed repel evil from those who believe. Allah indeed does not love any one who is unfaithful, ungrateful.
translations.22:39: Permission to fight back is given to those who are wrongfully attacked. And indeed, Allah is certainly capable of helping them.
translations.22:40: Those that have been displaced from their homes unjustly just because they say, “Our Lord is Allah.” And had Allah not withstood some with others among mankind, certainly then monastries and churches and synagogues and masajid, in which Allah's name is much remembered, would have been pulled down. And surely Allah will help him who helps His cause. Certainly indeed, Allah is Strong, Powerful.
translations.22:41: Those who will establish prayer, spend in charity, enjoin good and forbid evil, once We give them a foothold in the land. And with Allah is the end of all matters.
translations.22:42: And if they reject you, then so did the people of Noah and AAad and Thamood, before them, reject.
translations.22:43: And the people of Abraham and the people of Lot.
translations.22:44: And those that lived in Madyan (Midian in Hebrew). And Moses was rejected. I let the suppressors of Truth enjoy for a while, and then I seized them. And how severe in effect was My forsaking them!
translations.22:45: And many a town/village We did destroy when it committed injustices. And it lay in ruins with its roofs coming down. And many a well and lofty castle were deserted.
translations.22:46: Why do they not so conduct themselves on the earth as to have minds with which to understand, or ears with which to hear? For, surely, it is not the eyes that are blind, but it is the minds that are in the heads, which are blind.
translations.22:47: And they ask you to hasten with the punishment; and Allah is not the One to fail in His promise. And, indeed, a day with your Lord is a thousand years as you keep count of time.
translations.22:48: And how many an unjust town/village had I been indulgent to and patient with! And then I seized it!! And to Me is the final destination.
translations.22:49: Say, “O people! I am here only to give you a plain warning.”
translations.22:50: Those then that believe and do good deeds – they shall have forgiveness and a liberal provision.
translations.22:51: And those who strive to get the better of (or defeat the purpose of) Our Verses/signs – they shall be the inmates of the Hell.
translations.22:52: And no Messenger, nor Prophet, did we send before you, but when he delivered the divine Message to the people, the Satan cast doubts into that Message. And Allah blots out that which the Satan casts, and then does Allah make His Verses/signs prevail. And Allah is Knowledgeable, Wise.
translations.22:53: So that He may make what the Satan casts a temptation for those in whose hearts is a disease, and the means for hardening their hearts. And indeed, the wrongdoers are in a state of extreme rebellion.
translations.22:54: And so that those endowed with the knowledge know that it is the truth from your Lord, and so they believe in it and their hearts do humbly submit to it. And Allah is certainly indeed the Guide, of those who believe, towards a straight path.
translations.22:55: And those who suppress the Truth shall not cease to be in doubt concerning it until, suddenly, the Hour comes upon them, or, there comes upon them the torment of a day, any remedial action wherein would be fruitless.
translations.22:56: The sovereignty that day shall be absolutely Allah's; He will judge between them. And those who believe and do good deeds will be in gardens of bliss. 22:57 And those who suppress the Truth and reject Our Verses/signs – for those will there be a disgraceful punishment.
translations.22:58: And those who migrate in Allah's way and are then killed, or they die, them shall Allah give the best of provisions. And indeed, Allah it is Who is the Best of the providers.
translations.22:59: He will certainly admit them to a place which they shall be well pleased with. And Allah is indeed Knowledgeable, Gentle and Kind.
translations.22:60: So shall that be! And Allah surely helps the one that retaliates to the extent one was wronged but then an excess is committed. Allah is certainly indeed Lenient, Forgiving.
translations.22:61: That is because Allah causes the night to enter the day and causes the day to enter into the night, and because Allah hears, sees.
translations.22:62: That is because Allah is the Truth, and that what they pray to besides Him – that is the falsehood. And because, Allah is the One that is high, the One that is great.
translations.22:63: Don’t you see that Allah sends down water from the skies and then the earth becomes green? Allah is indeed meticulous, well-informed.
translations.22:64: His it is whatsoever there is in the heavens and whatsoever there is in the earth! And Allah indeed it is that surely is the Self-sufficient One, the Praised One.
translations.22:65: Don’t you see that Allah has made everything in the earth subservient to you? And the ship sails on the sea by His command. And He keeps the heavens from falling on the earth, except with His leave. Allah is indeed Kind and Merciful to men.
translations.22:66: And He it is Who has brought you to life, He will then cause you to die, and then again He will bring you to life. Certainly, indeed man is ungrateful.
translations.22:67: For every community We devised rites for them to observe. They should not therefore raise a dispute with you in the matter. And do invite them to your Lord! You are indeed on right guidance.
translations.22:68: And if they quarrel with you, say, “Allah knows what you do.”
translations.22:69: Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection on matters in which you differ.
translations.22:70: Don’t you know that Allah knows what is there in the heaven and the earth? That is indeed recorded in a book. That is indeed easy for Allah.
translations.22:71: And they worship, other than Allah, that for which He has sent no authority, and about which they have no knowledge. And no help shall avail those who indulge in wrong-doing.
translations.22:72: And when Our clear Verses are read out to them you will find denial in the faces of those who suppress the Truth. They well-nigh assault those who read out Our Verses to them. Say, “Shall I tell you what is worse than this? The Fire! Allah has promised it to those who suppress the Truth. And it’s a horrible place to go to!”
translations.22:73: O mankind! An example is given; listen to it: Those, whom you pray to besides Allah, cannot create a fly, even though they combine for the purpose. And if the fly snatches away something from them, they have no power to get that something released from the fly. Weak are the seeker and the sought, both!
translations.22:74: They have not given Allah the Credit due to Him! Allah is indeed Strong, Mighty.
translations.22:75: Allah chooses Messengers from among the angels and from among mankind. Allah does indeed hear, He does indeed see.
translations.22:76: HE knows what is before them and what is behind them. And to Allah are all matters returned.
translations.22:77: O you who believe! Bow and prostrate to and worship your Lord. And do that which is good so that you may achieve success.
translations.22:78: And strive in Allah’s way, as one ought to strive in His way. HE has chosen you [the pronoun is in plural] and has not laid upon you any severity in following the way of life approved by Him for you – the lifestyle of your forefather Abraham. HE named you as the Muslims before and in this (Qur’aan), so that the Messenger may be a witness over you, and you may be witnesses over mankind. Hence, pray to Allah regularly and properly, give charity and hold fast unto Him! HE is your Guardian. How excellent the Guardian, and how excellent the Supporter!
translations.23:2: Those that remain humble in their prayers,
translations.23:5: And those that guard their chastity,
translations.23:6: Except from their spouses or from those whom they rightfully possess. For indulging in sex with them, they are, of course, not to be blamed.
translations.23:7: But whoever seeks to go beyond that – those are the ones that go beyond limits.
translations.23:8: And those who are true to their trusts and who keep their word,
translations.23:9: And those who guard their prayers,
translations.23:10: They are the ones who shall inherit.
translations.23:11: They shall inherit the Paradise. And they shall be therein forever.
translations.23:12: And We did create the human being from an extract of clay.
translations.23:13: Then We made him/her a small seed (fertilized ovum) dropped into a resting-place, secure and compact.
translations.23:14: Then We made the seed a clinging leech-like clot of blood, then We made it a thing looking like a chewed morsel of food, then We made it bones, then We dressed the bones with flesh, and then We caused it to grow into another creature. Blessed then is Allah, the Best of the creators!
translations.23:15: Then, after that, you will indeed certainly die.
translations.23:16: Then, on the day of Resurrection, you shall indeed be raised from the dead.
translations.23:17: And We did make above you seven layers. And We have not ever been heedless about what We have created.
translations.23:18: And We send down water from the sky in measured quantities, then We cause it to settle down in the earth. And We can indeed certainly make it evaporate.
translations.23:19: Then We bring up thereby gardens of date-palm and grapes for you. There are plenty of fruits for you to eat, in those gardens.
translations.23:20: And a tree (olive) that grows out around Mount Sinai and produces oil and condiment for those who use these in their food.
translations.23:21: And certainly indeed there is a pointer for you in the cattle. We provide for you a drink out of what their bellies contain. And you have in them many benefits. And from them you get meat to eat.
translations.23:22: And on them and on the ships you are carried.
translations.23:23: And We did certainly send Noah to his people. And he said, “O my people! Worship Allah, you have no god other than Him. Will you not then fear Him?”
